blocks:
Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ زَلْزَلَةَ ٱلسَّاعَةِ شَىْءٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱)
O mankind! Be wary of your Lord! Indeed the quake of the Hour is a terrible thing. (1)

Key Points

| We read in narrations: In the month of Sha'ban in the sixth year of the Hijra, while the Muslims were marching towards the Battle of "Banu Mustaliq," the first and second verses of Surah Al-Hajj were revealed. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله ordered a halt; the Muslims stopped, and the حضرت recited these two verses. Some of the Prophet's companions spent that night weeping and expressing aversion to the world, but the Messenger of Allah calmed them with glad tidings. (17)

17) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- The addressees of the Quran are all people. «یا ایّها النّاس»

2- One must speak frankly with the heedless and warn them. «اتّقوا... انّ زلزلة الساعة»

3- The path to salvation from the dangers of the Day of Judgment is piety (Taqwa). «اتّقوا... زلزلة الساعة»

4- Remembering the Day of Judgment is a factor that creates piety (Taqwa). «اتّقوا... زلزلة الساعة»

5 - The entire existence is a meager provision, but the earthquake of the Day of Judgment is great. «زلزلة الساعة شى ء عظیم»

6- We should fear Allah, Who is the Lord and Master of the universe. «اتّقوا ربكم اِنّ زلزلة ...»

7- The existing order of the universe will collapse due to an earthquake. «اِنّ زلزلة الساعة شى ءعظیم»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-Hajj This Surah has seventy-eight verses and was revealed in Medina. The word "Hajj" linguistically means the intention or purpose of performing an action, but in Islamic Sharia, it refers to the specific rituals performed annually in Mecca. Since about thirteen verses of this Surah (from verse 25 to 37) speak about the Ka'bah, its history, and the political and social effects of Hajj, this Surah has been named "Al-Hajj." Of course, this Surah also discusses the call of divine prophets to many nations and relates their destinies. According to narrations, the Noble Prophet of Islam said: Whoever recites Surah Al-Hajj, Allah will grant him the reward of Hajj and Umrah equal to the number of people who have performed Hajj and Umrah in the past and future. (16)

Ayah

يَوْمَ تَرَوْنَهَا تَذْهَلُ كُلُّ مُرْضِعَةٍ عَمَّآ أَرْضَعَتْ وَتَضَعُ كُلُّ ذَاتِ حَمْلٍ حَمْلَهَا وَتَرَى ٱلنَّاسَ سُكَـٰرَىٰ وَمَا هُم بِسُكَـٰرَىٰ وَلَـٰكِنَّ عَذَابَ ٱللَّهِ شَدِيدٌ (۲)
The day that you will see it, every suckling female will be unmindful what she suckled, and every pregnant female will deliver her burden, and you will see the people drunk, yet they will not be drunken, but Allah’s punishment is severe. (2)

Key Points

The Qur'an uses startling expressions when describing the visage of the Day of Judgment, including: «یوماً یَجعل الوِلدان شَیبا» (18) a Day that turns children gray. «یوماً عَبوساً قَمطریرا» (19) a Day that is grim and hard. «یوماً كان شَرّه مُستطیرا» (20) a Day whose torment is widespread. The bond between a mother and her child is the strongest of bonds. Therefore, Allah, in describing the severity of the Day of Judgment, gives the example of the severing of the relationship between a nursing mother and a pregnant mother and her child. Otherwise, there will be no pregnant or nursing mothers on the Day of Judgment.

(18) Al-Muzzammil, 17. (19) Al-Insan, 10. (20) Al-Insan, 7.

Messages

1- The severe events of the Day of Judgment remove both affection, «تَذهَل كلّ مرضعة» (every nursing mother shall forget), and reason. «تَرىَ الناس سُكارى » (you will see the people intoxicated).

2- When explaining educational matters, we should use tangible and emotional examples. «مُرضعة... ذات حمل» (nursing mother... pregnant woman).

3- An analogy does not necessarily have to correspond to reality. «كلّ ذات حَمل... تَرىَ الناس سُكارى » (every pregnant woman... you will see the people intoxicated).

4- The analogies of the Qur'an do not become obsolete with the passage of time. «كلّ ذات حمل ...»

5- The cause of intoxication is not only wine. (Sometimes, arrogance, heedlessness, wealth, and status also become means of losing moderation.) «ترىَ النّاس سُكارى ... عذاب اللّه شدید» (you will see the people intoxicated... the punishment of Allah is severe).

6- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is protected and immune from the dangers of the Day of Judgment. «ترىَ النّاس سُكارى » (you will see the people intoxicated).

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يُجَـٰدِلُ فِى ٱللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَيَتَّبِعُ كُلَّ شَيْطَـٰنٍ مَّرِيدٍ (۳)
Among the people are those who dispute about Allah without any knowledge, and follow every froward devil, (3)

Key Points

| Marīd means devoid of goodness. A barren desert (devoid of vegetation) is called Marīd, and a beardless youth is called Amrad. What this Ayah prohibits is debate and contention without possessing the necessary knowledge. Otherwise, contention with opponents, if based on sound logic or even their own logic, is highly appropriate. As He says in another Ayah: «جادلهم بالّتى هىَ أحسن» (21)

(21) Nahl, 125.

Messages

1- The punishment of the Day of Judgment targets those who harm people's religious beliefs by creating doubts and skepticism. «عذاب اللّه شدید - و من النّاس مَن یُجادل»

2- Discussions must be based on logic and knowledge, not contention and fanaticism. «یُجادل... بغیرعلم»

3- Debate and contention without knowledge is following Satan. «یُجادل ... یتّبع»

4- Ignorance and fanaticism prevent the acceptance of the truth. «بغیر علم»

5- Whoever separates himself from Allah and His path is constantly trapped in the snare of a devil. «یتّبع كلّ شیطان»

6- Devils are numerous, and each one is a means of misleading people in some way. «كلّ شیطان»

7- There is no goodness in following Satan. «مَرید»

Ayah

كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِ أَنَّهُۥ مَن تَوَلَّاهُ فَأَنَّهُۥ يُضِلُّهُۥ وَيَهْدِيهِ إِلَىٰ عَذَابِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (٤)
about whom it has been decreed that he will mislead those who take him for an ally, and conduct them toward the punishment of the Blaze. (4)

Key Points

In the meaning of "كُتِبَ" (it was decreed), there is no compulsion (jabr) against Satan, because in other verses we read that Satan, by his own will and obstinacy against the truth, did not prostrate, objected to the action of Allah, and swore that he would mislead people. [22] Therefore, "كُتِب علیه" means that based on the will and decision that Satan made, misleading his followers has become certain for him.

22) Hijr, 31 to 40.

Messages

1- Choosing the path of Satan and following him yields no result except the certain divine punishment. "كتب علیه" (This is the certain will of Allah regarding the punishment of sinners, subsequent to their commission of the crime).

2- Following Satan is accepting his guardianship (wilayah). "و یتّبع كلّ شیطان... مَن تولاّه"

3- Satan only misleads those who follow him, not whomever he wishes. "مَن تولاّه فانّه یضلّه"

4- All satans lead humanity to one path: the blazing fire. "یتّبع كلّ شیطان... و یهدیه الى عذاب السعیر"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِن كُنتُمْ فِى رَيْبٍ مِّنَ ٱلْبَعْثِ فَإِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ ثُمَّ مِن مُّضْغَةٍ مُّخَلَّقَةٍ وَغَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ لِّنُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ ۚ وَنُقِرُّ فِى ٱلْأَرْحَامِ مَا نَشَآءُ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ثُمَّ نُخْرِجُكُمْ طِفْلًا ثُمَّ لِتَبْلُغُوٓا۟ أَشُدَّكُمْ ۖ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُتَوَفَّىٰ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰٓ أَرْذَلِ ٱلْعُمُرِ لِكَيْلَا يَعْلَمَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عِلْمٍ شَيْـًٔا ۚ وَتَرَى ٱلْأَرْضَ هَامِدَةً فَإِذَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَآءَ ٱهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ وَأَنۢبَتَتْ مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍۭ بَهِيجٍ (٥)
O people! If you are in doubt about the resurrection, [consider that] We created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then from a clinging mass, then from a fleshy tissue, partly formed and partly unformed, so that We may manifest [Our power] to you. We lodge in the wombs whatever [fetus] We wish for a specified term, then We bring you forth as infants, then [We rear you] so that you may come of age. [Then] there are some of you who are taken away, and there are some of you who are relegated to the nethermost age, such that he knows nothing after [having possessed] some knowledge. And you see the earth torpid, yet when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells, and grows every delightful kind [of plant]. (5)

Key Points

The word yutawaffā (یُتَوفّى), which stems from the root wafāt (وفاة), means the complete taking of the soul from the human existence and indicates the survival of the human soul after death. The word bahīj (بَهیج), from the root bahjah (بهجة), means freshness and lushness. Just as looking at greenery and plants causes delight (ibtihāj) and freshness for a person. Although in this Ayah, the period of old age and senescence is considered the lowest period of human life, the reason stated for it is not exclusive to the elderly. Rather, any time a person's intellectual faculties become weak and incapable, it is a period of decline, even if it occurs in youth. "أرذَل العُمُر... لایعلم مِن بَعد علمٍ شیئاً"

Messages

1- The source of doubt is heedlessness regarding the power of Allah. "اِن كنتم فى رَیب فانّا خَلقناكم"

2- We should speak to the disbelievers in a reasoned, logical, and understandable manner. "اِن كنتم فى رَیب... فانّا خَلقناكم"

3- Through sensory experiences, one can become familiar with intellectual concepts. "اِن كنتم فى رَیب... فانّا خَلقناكم"

4- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is both physical and spiritual. "اِن كنتم فى رَیب من البَعث فانّا خَلقناكم"

5- The origin of man and his primary matter is from dust. "خَلقناكم من تُراب"

6- Before death, man passes through seven stages. (Dust, sperm drop, clinging substance, chewed lump, infancy, maturity, old age). "خلقناكم من تُراب ثمّ من نُطفة ..."

7- The muḍghah stage (when man is in the mother's womb in the form of a lump of flesh) is the stage of human formation. "مُضغة مُخَلّقة"

8- He who created man from lifeless dust in this world can certainly do so on the Day of Resurrection. "لنُبیّن لكم"

9- Life has two curves: ascent and descent. The ascent is from dust until reaching the stage of "أشدّكم" (full strength), and the descent curve is reaching the stage of "أرذل العُمُر" (the lowest stage of life).

10- All stages of creation, from the settling of the sperm drop to the birth of the child, are in the hands of Allah. "خلقنا - نُقرّ فى الارحام - نُخرجكم طفلاً"

11- Natural transformations require time. (The word thumma (ثمّ), which denotes a time interval, is repeated several times in the Ayah).

12- The settling of the sperm drop or its miscarriage is by the will of Allah. "نُقِرّ فى الارحام ما نشاء"

13- The duration of pregnancy is timed by Allah. "الى أجل مسمّى"

14- The birth of man is by the will of Allah. "نُخرجكم"

15- All human beings are uniform on the first day: "طفلاً" (a child, singular), not "اطفالا" (children, plural). Later, due to nutrition and upbringing, each acquires specific morals and behaviors.

16- Death is not exclusive to the elderly. "و منكم مَن یُتوفّى"

17- Man is not annihilated by death. "یُتوفّى"

18- The value of man and his life lies in his knowledge, awareness, and intellect. (The Quran considers the reason for the lowest stage of life to be the forgetting of knowledge and scientific accumulations). "أرذل العُمُر لكیلا یعلم من بعد علم شیئاً"

19- Paired existence (male and female) is also relevant in plants. "من كلّ زوجٍ بَهیج"

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَأَنَّهُۥ يُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَأَنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٦)
All that is because Allah is the Reality and it is He who revives the dead, and He has power over all things, (6)
وَأَنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ ءَاتِيَةٌ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهَا وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَبْعَثُ مَن فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ (۷)
and because the Hour is bound to come, there is no doubt in it, and Allah will resurrect those who are in the graves. (7)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- The creation of man and vegetation is a sign of Allah's reality (or truth). «ذلك بانّ اللّه هوالحقّ»

2- Allah never abandons creation to death and mortality, because one who is the Truth (al-Haqq) never commits a vain or futile act. Therefore, He must bring His creations to their final destination and goal, and there must be another stage after death. «بانّ اللّه هوالحقّ و انّه یُحیى الموتى»

3- He who created once can create again. «یُحیى المَوتى»

4- He who both creates initially and can perform any kind of disposal (or action) upon His creation is the Absolute Omnipotent. «على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

5- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (corporeal). «یَبعثُ مَن فى القبور»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يُجَـٰدِلُ فِى ٱللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَلَا هُدًى وَلَا كِتَـٰبٍ مُّنِيرٍ (۸)
Among the people are those who dispute concerning Allah without any knowledge or guidance, or an enlightening Book, (8)

Messages

1- We should strive not to rebuke everyone when criticizing. «و من النّاس»

2- Argumentation (Jidal) is permissible if it is based on knowledge. «یُجادل... بغیر علم»

3- The paths to human knowledge are numerous: sometimes it is attained through thought, intellect, and study, which perhaps the word «بِغَیر علم» refers to; sometimes it is inspired to the heart, which perhaps the word «هُدى» refers to; and sometimes through heavenly books, revelation, and prophethood. «ولا كتاب مُنیر»

Ayah

ثَانِىَ عِطْفِهِۦ لِيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ لَهُۥ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا خِزْىٌ ۖ وَنُذِيقُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ عَذَابَ ٱلْحَرِيقِ (۹)
turning aside disdainfully to lead [others] astray from the way of Allah. For such there is disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We will make him taste the punishment of the burning: (9)

Key Points

The word thānī (ثانِى) means to twist, and ‘iṭf (عِطف) means the side (flank). Twisting the side is a metaphor for arrogance and pride.

Messages

1- The source of the dispute among the ignorant is pride and arrogance. «ثانِىَ عِطفه»

2- Punishment is proportionate to the sin. The punishment for the arrogant is humiliation. «فى الدّنیا خِزى»

3- The arrogant person will have a bad end in this very world. «فى الدّنیا خِزى»

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاكَ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّـٰمٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ (۱٠)
‘That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to the servants.’ (10)
وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَعْبُدُ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ حَرْفٍ ۖ فَإِنْ أَصَابَهُۥ خَيْرٌ ٱطْمَأَنَّ بِهِۦ ۖ وَإِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ فِتْنَةٌ ٱنقَلَبَ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهِۦ خَسِرَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْخُسْرَانُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱۱)
And among the people are those who worship Allah on the [very] fringe: if good fortune befalls him, he is content with it; but if an ordeal visits him he makes a turnabout, to become a loser in the world and the Hereafter. That is manifest loss. (11)

Key Points

Perhaps the word ظَلاّم (greatly unjust) is used in the noble Ayah because even a small injustice, when committed by the Just Allah, is considered a great injustice.

Messages

1- Allah is Just, and His wrath or mercy is the result of our actions. ذلك بما قَدّمت

2- We should not consider others the cause of our sins and transgressions, for whatever happens originates from ourselves. قَدّمت یَداك

3- The faith of some people is seasonal and superficial, and bitter and sweet events change it. و من النّاس ...

4- The account of belief and action based on logic is separate from the account of material ups and downs. (We should not seek religion for the sake of livelihood/material gain.) فَاِن اَصابَه خَیر...

5- Humanity is exposed to the onslaught of bitter and sweet events. اَصابَه ... اَصابته

6- Poverty and hardships are means of trial (testing). اَصابته فِتنة

7- Apostasy and turning away from the path of Allah lead to severe Divine wrath and the torment of Hellfire. اِنقَلَب على وَجهه خَسِرالدّنیا و الآخره

Ayahs

يَدْعُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَضُرُّهُۥ وَمَا لَا يَنفَعُهُۥ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلضَّلَـٰلُ ٱلْبَعِيدُ (۱۲)
He invokes besides Allah that which can bring him neither benefit nor harm. That is extreme error. (12)
يَدْعُوا۟ لَمَن ضَرُّهُۥٓ أَقْرَبُ مِن نَّفْعِهِۦ ۚ لَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَوْلَىٰ وَلَبِئْسَ ٱلْعَشِيرُ (۱۳)
He invokes someone whose harm is surely likelier than his benefit. An evil ally indeed, and an evil companion! (13)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- All profit and loss are in the hands of Allah, and nothing besides Him plays any role. «دونِ اللّه ما لایَضُرّه و ما لا یَنفَعه»

2- Polytheism (Shirk) and attachment to anything other than Allah is the greatest deviation. «دون اللّه ... هوالضّلال البعید»

3- Polytheism lacks any rational or logical basis. «دونِ اللّه مالا یَضُرّه» Because worship is either for attaining a benefit or preventing a harm, neither of which idols possess.

4- Polytheism, in all its forms (whether the worshipped entities are rational or irrational), is condemned. (Once, the word «ما» [what] was used alongside «یَدعوا» [he calls], which refers to non-sentient beings, and once, the word «مَن» [who] was used alongside the phrase «یَدعو», which refers to sentient worshipped entities such as humans, angels, and jinn.)

5- We should not trade the permanent harm of the Hereafter for the fleeting benefits of this world. «ضَرّه أقرَب مِن نفعه»

6- The harm caused by the tyrants (Taghuts) to the people is greater than whatever they give them. «ضَرّه أقرب من نفعه»

7- Do not associate with someone who turns you away from Allah and invites you toward himself, and do not take him as your guardian or leader. «لبئس المولى»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يُرِيدُ (۱٤)
Allah will indeed admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them. Indeed Allah does whatever He desires. (14)

Messages

1- Faith and hope in the true promises are the greatest factors for abandoning all besides Him. «اِنّ اللّه یُدخِل ...»

2- Faith accompanied by action is the key to salvation, and neither one alone is effective. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

3- The diversity of divine blessings is for the greater enjoyment of the inhabitants of Paradise. «جنّات، اَنهار»

4- Allah rewards based on human inclinations. (Man enjoys gardens and rivers; in Paradise, there are also gardens and rivers). «جنّات، اَنهار»

5- Allah is the Omnipotent, and in carrying out what He wills, He reaches no obstacle or dead end. «یَفعل مایُرید» (This very power guarantees the fulfillment of His promises).

Ayah

مَن كَانَ يَظُنُّ أَن لَّن يَنصُرَهُ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ بِسَبَبٍ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ ثُمَّ لْيَقْطَعْ فَلْيَنظُرْ هَلْ يُذْهِبَنَّ كَيْدُهُۥ مَا يَغِيظُ (۱٥)
Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the ceiling and hang himself, and see if his artifice would remove his rage. (15)

Key Points

Some have not considered the Ayah specific to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and have translated it thus: Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him... Perhaps the meaning of the Ayah is that even space travel does not bring peace to the godless person. The phrase «سبب الى السماء» refers to the means of space travel, and the word «لیقطع» means traversing the path (or cutting off the path). In this case, this Ayah is one of the scientific miracles and prophecies of the Quran. In any case, humanity's spiritual problem is not solved even with advanced technology and space travel. «هل یُذهبنّ كَیده ما یغیظ»

Messages

1- Divine aid and victory are certain in this world and the Hereafter. «یَظنّ أن لن یَنصُره اللّه فى الدّنیا والآخرة»

2- Despair of Allah's power and victory destroys a person's balance. «یَظنّ أن لن یَنصُره اللّه»

3- Suicide is never a path to salvation. «هل یُذهِبنّ كَیدُه ما یَغیظ»

(The only means of tranquility is faith and reliance upon Allah, and any other plan or program, minus His Divine will and faith in Him, is futile.)

4- Utilizing the earth and the sky, ascent and descent, and any action lacking faith, is ineffective. «هل یُذهبنّ كَیدُه ما یَغیظ»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَهْدِى مَن يُرِيدُ (۱٦)
Thus have We sent it down as manifest signs, and indeed Allah guides whomever He desires. (16)

Messages

1- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is based on sending down signs (Ayat) and means of guidance. «كذلك اَنزلناه»

2- The Quran is a book of light, and its verses are clear. «بیّنات» (Contrary to the view of some who say: The Quran is definite in its issuance [Qat'i al-Sudur] but probabilistic in its meaning [Zanni al-Dalalah].)

3- The Quran is a means of guidance, but the essence of guidance is the work of Allah and His grace. «أنّ اللّه یَهدى»

4- Allah guides whomever He wills, but His will is subject to His wisdom, and therefore He guides only those who have prepared the necessary grounds within themselves. «یَهدى مَن یُرید»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ وَٱلصَّـٰبِـِٔينَ وَٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ وَٱلْمَجُوسَ وَٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (۱۷)
Indeed Allah will indeed judge between the faithful, the Jews, the Sabaeans, the Christians, the Magians and the polytheists on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allah is witness to all things. (17)

Key Points

There is much discussion regarding who the Sabeans (Sabi'in) are. Some have said they are star-worshippers, some consider them worshippers of angels, and others say they are a group of the followers of Noah عليه السلام. A person asked Hadrat Ali عليه السلام: Why is Jizya (a type of tax) taken from the Magians (Majus), even though they had neither a Prophet nor a Book, and the taking of Jizya is specific to the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitab)? The Imam replied: The Magians also possess a heavenly Book and a divine Prophet. [23] In this Ayah, a distinction is made between the groups using the word al-ladhīna (الّذین). Similarly, on the Day of Resurrection, the reckoning of the true believers will be separate from the reckoning of the misguided polytheists, and both will be separate from the reckoning of the followers of other heavenly religions. The word al-ladhīna (الّذین) is used three times: regarding the believers, regarding the followers of other religions, and regarding the polytheists.

23) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 306.

Messages

1- When naming the groups, the right of the true believers is given precedence. «الّذین آمنوا»

2- Judaism, Christianity, Magianism (Zoroastrianism), and Sabeanism are all monotheistic and heavenly religions, because their names are mentioned in contrast to the polytheists. «والّذین هادوا... والّذین اَشركوا»

3- The Resurrection is for everyone, and on that Day, judgment will be passed between all groups, and all disputes will cease. «اِنّ اللّه یَفصِل بینهم»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, Allah is both the Judge and the Witness. «اِنّ اللّه یَفصِل - اِنّ اللّه على كلّ شى ء شهید»

5- A judgment is complete only when the judge possesses comprehensive information. «یَفصِل ... على كلّ شى ء شهید»

6- Inviting to Islam and engaging in goodly argumentation is necessary, but do not expect the cessation of religious differences in this world. Maintain peaceful coexistence in this world until Allah passes the final judgment on the Day of Resurrection. «اِنّ اللّه یَفصِل بینهم یوم القیامة»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَسْجُدُ لَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ وَٱلنُّجُومُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ وَٱلشَّجَرُ وَٱلدَّوَآبُّ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۗ وَمَن يُهِنِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن مُّكْرِمٍ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۩ (۱۸)
Have you not regarded that whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth prostrates to Allah, as well as the sun, the moon, and the stars, the mountains, the trees, and the animals and many humans? And many have come to deserve the punishment. Whomever Allah humiliates will find no one who may bring him honour. Indeed Allah does whatever He wishes. (18)

Messages

1- There are conscious beings in the heavens. «مَن فِى السموات» (The word «مَن» is used for conscious beings.)

2- The entire existence prostrates (sajdah) and submits to Allah. «یَسجُد» And consciousness is not exclusive to humans. «مَن فى السموات و الارض» (If we know that all existence is submissive to Allah, we will not be a discordant element. Polytheism (shirk) and arrogance are contrary to the order of existence.)

3- Man is a being of choice. «یَسجُد... كثیرٌ من النّاس و كثیرٌ حقّ علیه العذاب»

4- Divine punishments are based on truth and justice. «حَقَّ علیه العذاب»

5- Honor and disgrace are solely in the hands of Allah. «مَن یُهِن اللّه فما له من مُكرِم»

6- There is no obstacle against the will of Allah. «یفعل ما یشاء»

Ayah

۞ هَـٰذَانِ خَصْمَانِ ٱخْتَصَمُوا۟ فِى رَبِّهِمْ ۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ قُطِّعَتْ لَهُمْ ثِيَابٌ مِّن نَّارٍ يُصَبُّ مِن فَوْقِ رُءُوسِهِمُ ٱلْحَمِيمُ (۱۹)
These two contending groups contend concerning their Lord. As for those who are faithless, cloaks of fire will be cut out for them, and boiling water will be poured over their heads, (19)

Messages

1- Throughout history, conflict has centered on Allah. «اِختَصَموا فى ربّهم»

2- The garments of every inhabitant of Hell are tailored specifically for his body. «قُطّعَت لهم ثیاب»

3- The fiery garments of the inhabitants of Hell will not cool down, and molten material is constantly poured upon them from above their heads. «یُصَبّ من فوق رُؤسهم الحَمیم»

Ayahs

يُصْهَرُ بِهِۦ مَا فِى بُطُونِهِمْ وَٱلْجُلُودُ (۲٠)
with which their skins and entrails will fuse, (20)
وَلَهُم مَّقَـٰمِعُ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ (۲۱)
and there will be clubs of iron for them. (21)

Key Points

|

Ayah

كُلَّمَآ أَرَادُوٓا۟ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنْهَا مِنْ غَمٍّ أُعِيدُوا۟ فِيهَا وَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْحَرِيقِ (۲۲)
Whenever they desire to leave it out of anguish, they will be turned back into it [and told]: ‘Taste the punishment of the burning!’ (22)

Messages

1- The Hellfire burns both the inside and the outside. «بُطونِهم - والجُلود»

2- The inhabitants of Hell struggle desperately for their rescue, but their efforts are fruitless. «أرادوا أن یَخرُجوا منها - اُعیدوا فیها»

3- More painful than the Hellfire are the sorrows and spiritual torments. «من غَمّ»

4- The torment of Hell is eternal. «اُعیدوا فیها»

5- The inhabitants of Hell are humiliated and reproached. «ذوقوا»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُهُمْ فِيهَا حَرِيرٌ (۲۳)
Indeed Allah will admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them, adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearl, and their dress therein will be silk. (23)

Messages

1- The best way to find joy and recreation is by utilizing nature. «جنّات - اَنهار»

2- Alongside warning, hope and good tidings are necessary. In the previous Ayah, He said: «ذوقوا عذاب الحَریق» (Taste the punishment of the Burning), and in this Ayah, He said: «لباسُهم فیها حَریر» (Their clothing therein will be silk).

3- One of the beauties of the Quran is the balance between its fears and hopes, its warnings and its good tidings. «قُطّعَت لهم ثیاب - و لباسهم فیها حَریر»

4- Nakedness is not a virtue; the people of Paradise also have clothing. «لباسهم فیها حَریر»

5- Allah compensates for deprivations. Silk and gold, which are forbidden for men in this world, will be a means of their adornment in the Hereafter. «ذَهَبٍ - حَریر»

6- The beauty and harmony of the words and phrases is a type of eloquence. (The last phrase of the previous Ayah was «حَریق» and the last phrase of this Ayah is «حَریر»).

Ayah

وَهُدُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلطَّيِّبِ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ وَهُدُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطِ ٱلْحَمِيدِ (۲٤)
They were guided to chaste belief, and guided to the path of the All-laudable. (24)

Messages

1- Alongside the material blessings of Paradise, there are spiritual blessings. «لباسُهم فیها حَریر... و هُدوا الى الطَیّب من القول»

2- Pleasant words are the gift of the people of Paradise. «الطَیّب من القول»

3- Traversing the path of Allah requires both outward and inward adornment. «اَساوِرَ من ذَهَب - الطَیّب من القول - صراط الحمید»

4- The best success is a good outcome. «هدوا الى صِراط الحَمید»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلْنَـٰهُ لِلنَّاسِ سَوَآءً ٱلْعَـٰكِفُ فِيهِ وَٱلْبَادِ ۚ وَمَن يُرِدْ فِيهِ بِإِلْحَادٍۭ بِظُلْمٍ نُّذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۲٥)
Indeed those who are faithless and bar from the way of Allah and the Sacred Mosque, which We have made for all people, the native and the visitor being equal therein—whoever wrongfully tries to commit violation in it, We shall make him taste a painful punishment. (25)

Key Points

| Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Whoever worships other than Allah in Al-Masjid Al-Haram, or appoints guardians for that sacred place other than the Awliya' (friends/representatives) of Allah, then he has committed oppression (zulm) and deviation (Ilhad). [24]

24) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 337.

Messages

1- Corrupt belief leads a person to corrupt action. «كفروا - یَصُدّون»

2- Ordinary sins become the prelude to greater sins. «یَصدّون عن سبیل اللّه و المسجدالحرام»

3- Religious centers are a thorn in the eye of the deviants. «یَصدّون عن... المسجدالحرام»

4- The laws of Mecca must be determined by the true Owner. «جَعلناه للنّاس سَواء...»

5- Al-Masjid Al-Haram is an international zone, and like the heavens and the oceans, it is not the right of any single individual, country, or government. «سَواءٌ العاكِف فیه و الباد»

6- Allah's punishment for the deviants is certain, and no disbeliever or oppressor is safe from the Divine chastisement. «و مَن یُرد»

7- Places are not equal. «و مَن یُرد فیه بالحاد...» (Al-Masjid Al-Haram must be a center of security for everyone).

8- Intentional and conscious deviation committed with the purpose of oppression is separate from unintentional deviation. «یَصُدّون - و مَن یُرِد» Therefore, the mere intention of Ilhad (deviation) warrants punishment. «نُذِقْه»

9- Insulting sanctities and religious centers warrants chastisement. «نُذِقه من عذاب»

Ayah

وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنَا لِإِبْرَٰهِيمَ مَكَانَ ٱلْبَيْتِ أَن لَّا تُشْرِكْ بِى شَيْـًٔا وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِىَ لِلطَّآئِفِينَ وَٱلْقَآئِمِينَ وَٱلرُّكَّعِ ٱلسُّجُودِ (۲٦)
When We settled for Abraham the site of the House [saying], Do not ascribe any partners to Me, and purify My House for those who circle around it, and those who stand [in it for prayer], and those who bow and prostrate themselves. (26)

Messages

1- The story of the Ka'bah and Abraham is an event that must not be forgotten. «و اذ»

2- All of us are in need of Divine success (Tawfiqat). «بَوّأنا»

3- Sacred places must be placed under the authority of sacred individuals. «بَوّأنا لابراهیم مكان البیت»

4- People around the world knew the Ka'bah from ancient times. «البیت»

5- No form of polytheism (Shirk) is permissible towards Allah. «لا تُشرك بى شیئاً»

6- First inner purity (Tahara), then outer cleanliness. «لا تُشرك بى - طَهّر بَیتى»

7- Service to the mosque is an Abrahamic honor. «طَهِّر بَیتى»

8- Self-purification alone is not enough; service to creation is also necessary. «لاتُشرك بى... طَهِّر بیتى للطّائفین و...»

9- Wherever is attributed to Allah is sacred, and everyone must respect it. «طَهّر بَیتى»

10- The center of worship must be pure. «طَهّر بَیتى»

11- Prayer (Salat) and the one who prays are so honored that even Abraham must be the servant of its location. «طَهّر بَیتى للطّائفین... و الرُّكّع السؤجود»

12- The right (Haqq) of the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) is circumambulation (Tawaf), standing (Qiyam), and prayer (Salat); and among these, Tawaf has a greater reward. «للطّائفین» (The fact that Tawaf is mentioned before bowing [Ruku'] may indicate that in the Masjid al-Haram, Tawaf is more important than prayer.)

Ayah

وَأَذِّن فِى ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْحَجِّ يَأْتُوكَ رِجَالًا وَعَلَىٰ كُلِّ ضَامِرٍ يَأْتِينَ مِن كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍ (۲۷)
And proclaim the Hajj to all the people: they will come to you on foot and on lean camels, coming from distant places, (27)

Key Points

The word ḍāmir (ضامِر) means an animal whose fat has melted away and whose flesh has turned into muscle, making it agile, like an animal that has been conditioned (body-built) and is ready for competitions. Imam Ali عليه السلام states in Sermon 28 of Nahj al-Balagha: "ألیوم المِضمار و غَداً السّباق" (Today is the training ground, and tomorrow is the race), meaning, make yourselves agile today so that you can take the lead (or precede others) on the Day of Judgment. We read in narrations: Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام said: O God! My voice does not reach anyone. Allah said: You proclaim, I will convey it to the people. The first duty of the pilgrims of the Ka'bah is to wear the clothes of Ihrām and say "Labbayk Allāhumma Labbayk" (Here I am, O Allah, here I am). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: These phrases are the response to the invitation of Ibrahim. (25) The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله also commanded in Medina in the tenth year (of Hijra) that the mu'adhdhins (callers to prayer) invite the people with the loudest voice to perform Hajj alongside the Prophet. (26)

25) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Ilal al-Sharā’i‘, p. 416. 26) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 245.

Messages

1- The proclamation of Allah's saints today will affect future generations. "أذِّن - یَأتوك" (Proclaim - they will come to you).

2- The Prophets were pioneers in enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'rūf). "و أذِّن" (And proclaim).

3- Going for Hajj is reaching the presence of Allah's saints. "یَأتوك" (they will come to you). (He did not say: "یأتوه" [they will come to it]. Yes, the divine leader is the axis of the Hajj rituals.)

4- Allah informs about the unseen and the future of history. "یَأتوك - یَأتین" (they will come to you - they will come).

5- If a person is obligated to perform a task, he must prepare its prerequisites. "و على كلّ ضامر..." (and upon every lean mount...).

6- Going for Hajj on foot is superior to going mounted. (Rijālan [on foot] precedes kulli ḍāmir [every lean mount]).

7- If there is will and love in performing a task, resources come second. "رجالاً و على كلّ ضامر" (on foot and upon every lean mount).

8- Hajj is the only destination that always has travelers from all regions of the world. "من كلّ فَجّ عمیق" (from every deep ravine).

Ayah

لِّيَشْهَدُوا۟ مَنَـٰفِعَ لَهُمْ وَيَذْكُرُوا۟ ٱسْمَ ٱللَّهِ فِىٓ أَيَّامٍ مَّعْلُومَـٰتٍ عَلَىٰ مَا رَزَقَهُم مِّنۢ بَهِيمَةِ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ ۖ فَكُلُوا۟ مِنْهَا وَأَطْعِمُوا۟ ٱلْبَآئِسَ ٱلْفَقِيرَ (۲۸)
that they may witness the benefits for them, and mention Allah’s Name during the known days over the livestock He has provided them. So eat thereof and feed the destitute and the needy. (28)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "أیّاماً معلومات" (known days) is the Days of Tashreeq, which are the 11th, 12th, and 13th days of the month of Dhul Hijjah. [27] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "یُذكروا اسم اللّه" (mention the Name of Allah) is the recitation of Allahu Akbar and the phrases that follow it, which are recited in a well-known supplication after fifteen prayers, the first of which is the Dhuhr prayer on the Day of Eid al-Adha. [28] Imam Kadhim عليه السلام, in response to a person, said: There is no objection if the skin of the sacrifice in Mecca is given to the butcher as payment, because that which is edible must be consumed by oneself or by the poor, "كُلوا منها و اَطعِموا..." (Eat thereof and feed...), and the skin is not edible. [29]

Portrait of Hajj:

Hajj is a general mobilization and a maneuver of the monotheists. Hajj is the beautiful face of love and devotion. Hajj is the keeping alive of the memories and services of Prophets such as Ibrahim, Isma'il, and Muhammad. (May the peace of Allah be upon all of them) Hajj is the center of international Muslim gatherings. Hajj is the center for communication and the exchange of news and information in the Islamic world. Hajj is the economic backbone of Muslims and creates employment for tens of thousands of Muslims. Hajj is the best opportunity and time for Islamic propagation, exposing conspiracies, supporting the oppressed, declaring disavowal (Bara'ah) from the disbelievers, and instilling awe and fear in their hearts. Hajj is the best opportunity and time for repentance, remembering death and the Resurrection, cutting oneself off from everything, seeing the plains of Arafat and Mash'ar, and awaiting the Promised Mahdi عليه السلام.

27) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 297. 28) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 29) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Muslims must be present on the scene to attain the material and spiritual benefits of Hajj. «لِیَشهدوا»
  2. The benefits of Hajj are so important that even if people come from the farthest points of the world, it is still worthwhile. «فَجٍّ عمیق - لیَشهدوا»
  3. Performing the command of Allah and having the intention of seeking closeness (Qurbah) in Hajj does not contradict various secondary benefits. «لِیَشهدوا منافع لهم»
  4. Divine commands are for the benefit of the people. «منافع لهم» (benefits for them) is stated, not «منافعهم» (their benefits).
  5. In Hajj, there are both worldly and otherworldly benefits. ("منافع" (benefits) is mentioned absolutely, and Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "منافع" in the Ayah refers to the benefits of this world and the Hereafter.) [30]
  6. The philosophy of acts of worship is the remembrance of Allah. «و یُذكروا اسم اللّه» (and mention the Name of Allah). (Regarding the philosophy of prayer, we also read: «أقِم الصّلوة لِذكرى» meaning, establish prayer for My remembrance.)
  7. The element of time is effective in supplication and prayer. «فى أیّام مَعلومات» (in known days). (Islamic acts of worship have specific timings.)
  8. Livestock, their submission to humans, their sacrifice, and the other benefits they possess, are among the Divine blessings bestowed upon mankind. «رَزَقَهم» (He has provided them).
  9. It is permissible for the pilgrims themselves to use the meat of the sacrifice. «فكُلوا» (So eat). (This is contrary to the customs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), where they considered its consumption forbidden for the pilgrim.)
  10. Presence in Hajj and attaining its benefits must be accompanied by poverty alleviation. «واَطعِموا البائس» (and feed the distressed).
  11. The gathering of the wealthy in Hajj should not make them oblivious to the poor. «اَطعِموا البائس الفقیر» (feed the distressed, the poor).

30) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 47, p. 422.

Ayah

ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا۟ تَفَثَهُمْ وَلْيُوفُوا۟ نُذُورَهُمْ وَلْيَطَّوَّفُوا۟ بِٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْعَتِيقِ (۲۹)
Then let them do away with their untidiness, fulfill their vows, and circle around the Ancient House. (29)

Key Points

The word tafath (تَفث) means dirt, hair, nails, and mustache. Qada' al-tafath (قضاء تفث) means to exit the state of Ihrām (consecration) by trimming the nails and hair, and then performing the Tawāf (circumambulation) around the Ka'bah to continue the rites of Hajj. On the Day of Eid al-Qurbān (Eid al-Adha), three actions are obligatory for the pilgrims of the House of God in the land of Minā, near Mecca: 1. Throwing seven pebbles at the place of Satan, which is called Ramy al-Jamrah. 2. Slaughtering and sacrifice, which was mentioned in the previous Ayah. 3. Shaving the head or shortening the hair and nails, which is mentioned in this Ayah. Imam al-Bāqir عليه السلام said: The Ka'bah has neither an inhabitant nor an owner; rather, it is a free and independent House. [31]

31) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Hygiene and cleanliness are a prerequisite for entry in some acts of worship, such as prayer (Salāt); they are an integral part in others, such as Hajj; and they are considered a condition for the perfection of worship in cases like reciting the Qur'an and I'tikāf (spiritual retreat). «ثُمّ لْیَقضوا تَفَثهم»

  2. The tradition of removing dirt and eliminating contamination is a definite and serious command, not merely a recommendation. «ثمّ لْیَقضوا»

  3. Contaminations originate from the human being himself, «تَفَثهم», but benefits are a divine favor that has been established for humanity. «منافع لهم»

  4. To perform Tawāf around the sacred and free House, you must be free from contaminations and obligations. «ثمّ لیَقضوا - ولْیُوفوا - ولیَطّوفوا»

  5. Fulfilling a vow (Nadhir) is obligatory. «و لیوفوا»

  6. History (or antiquity) can be a value. «العَتیق» (The word al-'Atīq means ancient, and atīqah refers to ancient objects).

  7. The House of God is not the property of anyone. «العَتیق»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ وَمَن يُعَظِّمْ حُرُمَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦ ۗ وَأُحِلَّتْ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمُ إِلَّا مَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَٱجْتَنِبُوا۟ ٱلرِّجْسَ مِنَ ٱلْأَوْثَـٰنِ وَٱجْتَنِبُوا۟ قَوْلَ ٱلزُّورِ (۳٠)
That, and whoever venerates the sacraments of Allah, that is better for him with his Lord. You are permitted [animals of] grazing livestock except for what will be recited to you. So avoid the abomination of idols, and avoid false speech, (30)

Key Points

Many instances have been mentioned for qawl az-zūr (false speech), including: lying, false testimony, and ghinā (singing/music). Similarly, instances have been mentioned for ḥurumāt Allāh (the sanctities of Allah), such as the law of Allah, the Book of Allah, and the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Messenger of Allah علیهم السلام, the preservation of the sanctity of all of which is necessary. [32] False testimony has been placed in the rank of associating partners with Allah (Shirk), and qawl az-zūr is among the major sins. [33]

32) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqā’iq. 33) Tafsir Nūr ath-Thaqalayn; Bihār, Vol. 47, pp. 216 to 217.

Messages

1- We must honor that which Allah has held sacred. «و مَن یُعظّم حُرُمات اللّه» (We should mention divine commands with respect and reverence, and be active and sensitive in implementing them.)

2- Respect for divine laws and sanctities is for our own benefit. «فهو خیرٌ له» And the reward for this respect is with Him. «عند ربّه»

3- The default rule regarding livestock (quadrupeds) is that they are lawful (halal), unless we have a specific proof from revelation. «اُحِلّت - الاّ ما یُتلى»

4- Idol worship is filth/defilement. «فاجتَنِبوا الرّجس مِنَ الاَوثان» (The rituals of Hajj and sacrifice were mixed with polytheism and idol worship.) «فاجتَنِبوا»

Ayah

حُنَفَآءَ لِلَّهِ غَيْرَ مُشْرِكِينَ بِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ فَكَأَنَّمَا خَرَّ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَتَخْطَفُهُ ٱلطَّيْرُ أَوْ تَهْوِى بِهِ ٱلرِّيحُ فِى مَكَانٍ سَحِيقٍ (۳۱)
as persons having pure faith in Allah, not ascribing partners to Him. Whoever ascribes partners to Allah is as though he had fallen from a height, then [his corpse] is devoured by vultures, or [his remains are] blown away by the wind far and wide. (31)

Key Points

«حُنفاء» (Hunafā') is the plural of Hanīf, meaning someone who follows the true religion. «خَطَف» (Khaṭaf) means to snatch quickly, and «سَحیق» (Saḥīq) means distant. Monotheism (Tawhīd) gives value to everything, while polytheism (Shirk) strips value from the best of creatures. The hoopoe (Hudhud)—which believed in the Lord of the worlds—due to its fervor and desire to guide the polytheistic people, reached a status where it became the intermediary for the guidance of an entire region. But man, due to polytheism, falls so low that he becomes food for birds. The examples in the Quran never become obsolete in any time or place; they are both natural and understandable. «كانّما خَرّ من السماء» (as if he fell from the sky).

Messages

1- Sacrifice, circumambulation (Tawāf), fulfilling vows, and all deeds must be solely for Allah. «حُنفاء للّه» (sincerely devoted to Allah).

2- If we connect ourselves to any power other than Allah, the end result is a fall, even for superpowers. «ومَن یُشرك... خَرّ من السماء» (And whoever associates partners with Allah... falls from the sky).

3- Polytheism is unforgivable for anyone. «و مَن یُشرك» (And whoever associates partners).

4- Monotheism and sincerity are like being present in the sky, while polytheism is like falling from the sky and becoming prey for birds. «خَرّ من السماء» (falls from the sky).

5- There is no moment of distance between separating oneself from Allah, falling, and becoming prey for predators. «فَتَخطَفُه» (the letter fā' and the word khaṭaf).

6- The most sacred acts (Tawāf), in the most sacred places (Al-Masjid al-Ḥarām), and during the most sacred days (Eid al-Qurbān/Sacrifice), if not accompanied by sincerity, their outcome is a fall, not growth. «ومَن یُشرك... خَرّ من السماء».

7- Sincerity insures man against winds and storms, while lack of sincerity places man in the path of storms. «او تَهوى به الرِّیح» (or the wind sweeps him away).

8- Polytheism, even if minor or momentary, leaves no capital (asset) remaining. «خَرّ من السماء - فى مكان سَحیق» (falls from the sky - into a distant place).

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ وَمَن يُعَظِّمْ شَعَـٰٓئِرَ ٱللَّهِ فَإِنَّهَا مِن تَقْوَى ٱلْقُلُوبِ (۳۲)
That. And whoever venerates the sacraments of Allah—indeed that arises from the Godwariness of hearts. (32)

Key Points

The Holy Quran, in other verses, explicitly counts two rituals and programs of Hajj as part of the Divine Symbols (Sha'a'ir): one is "Safa and Marwah" and the other is "the sacrificial camels." However, the Divine Symbols are not limited to these two, and all collective acts of worship are among the Symbols of Allah, such as Friday and congregational prayers, and all Hajj rituals. Piety (Taqwa) is a spiritual state whose existence is inferred from its effects and signs. This is because, in outward actions, there is not much difference between sin and reward. For example, a sincere prayer and a hypocritical prayer are outwardly the same, and what makes one valuable and the other worthless is the spirit and inner reality of the action, which relates to the hearts. «تقوى القلوب» (Taqwa of the hearts).

Messages

1- Inner piety must also have external effects. Whoever disregards the Symbols (Sha'a'ir), his heart's piety is, in reality, deficient. «مَن یُعظِّم شعائر اللّه فانّها من تقوى القلوب»

2- Commemorations and honoring must be based on piety, not competition. «مَن یُعظِّم... تقوى القلوب»

Ayah

لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَـٰفِعُ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ثُمَّ مَحِلُّهَآ إِلَى ٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْعَتِيقِ (۳۳)
You may benefit from them until a specified time. Then their place of sacrifice is by the Ancient House. (33)

Key Points

Some people thought that once a camel or another animal was designated for sacrifice, they did not have the right to ride it or milk it. This Ayah rejects this notion.

Messages

1- In the shadow of honoring the rituals (Sha'a'ir), your material benefits are also secured. «و لكم فیها منافع»

2- In the Hajj ceremonies, the centrality lies with the Ka'bah. «ولیَطّوفّوا بالبیت... مَحِلّها الى البیت العتیق»

Ayah

وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ جَعَلْنَا مَنسَكًا لِّيَذْكُرُوا۟ ٱسْمَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَىٰ مَا رَزَقَهُم مِّنۢ بَهِيمَةِ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ ۗ فَإِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ فَلَهُۥٓ أَسْلِمُوا۟ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُخْبِتِينَ (۳٤)
For every nation We have appointed a rite so that they might mention Allah’s Name over the livestock He has provided them. Your God is the One God, so submit to Him. And give good news to the humble (34)

Key Points

The word mukhbitīn (مُخبتین) comes from khabt (خَبت), meaning a wide, level ground that is free of ups and downs. A mukhbit is referred to as a calm and confident person who is far removed from any notion of polytheism (shirk). The word mansak (مَنسَك) is either a verbal noun (masdar), a noun of time (ism zamān), or a noun of place (ism makān). Therefore, the meaning is: We appointed for every nation a program of worship, or a time for sacrifice, or a place for it.

Messages

  1. The practice of sacrifice has existed in all divine religions. «لكلّ اُمّة...»
  2. When sacrificing, the name of Allah must be uttered. «لیَذكُروا»
  3. The method of worshipping Allah must only be through revelation. «جَعلنا مَنسكاً»
  4. Our God is the One Lord. «فالهكم اله واحد», the same God Who provides for our religion, «جعلنا مَنسكاً», and for our worldly life, «رَزَقهم من بَهیمة الانعام».
  5. The Name of Allah is so valuable that if it is uttered when slaughtering an animal, the meat of that animal becomes lawful (halal); otherwise, it will be unlawful (haram). «لیَذكُروا اسم اللّه»
  6. The food of God-worshipping people must also have a divine color (aspect). «لیَذكُروا اسم اللّه»
  7. Do not submit except to Allah. «فله اَسلِموا»
  8. Since mentioning the Name of Allah is emphasized in all religions, the Creator of all is one. For if there were another god, these rituals would have been presented in other forms as well. «فالهكم اله واحد» (The letter fā’ (ف) indicates a conclusion, meaning the unity of the school (of thought) is a sign of the unity of the Worshipped One).
  9. Divine glad tidings are specific to those who are humble and submissive to Allah. «بَشّر المخبِتین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَصَابَهُمْ وَٱلْمُقِيمِى ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ (۳٥)
—those whose hearts tremble with awe when Allah is mentioned, and who are patient through whatever visits them, and who maintain the prayer and spend out of what We have provided them. (35)

Key Points

The remembrance of Allah is both comforting and fear-inducing for the people of faith, just like a child who, by remembering his parents, finds both peace and also holds them in awe and respects their authority.

Messages

1- The fear of Allah and inner awe are a virtue. «وَجِلت قلوبهم»

2- Piety (Taqwa) precedes all other perfections. «وَجِلت قلوبهم... والصّابرین ...»

3- The foundation for fulfilling one's duty, after piety, is steadfastness and patience. «وَجِلت قلوبهم والصّابرین»

4- Patience is valuable only if it endures in the face of difficulties. «الصابرین على مااصابهم»

5- The relationship with Allah is inseparable from the relationship with the deprived. «والمقیمى الصلوة - ینفقون»

6- Giving is not exclusive to wealth; one can also spend (infaq) from knowledge, reputation, and skill. «ممّا رزقناهم»

7- Spending (infaq) is valuable if it is continuous. («ینفقون» is a present tense verb indicating continuity)

8- Moderation and temperance are also necessary in spending (infaq). «و ممّا...»

9- Human wealth and possessions are Divine provision. «رزقناهم»

Ayah

وَٱلْبُدْنَ جَعَلْنَـٰهَا لَكُم مِّن شَعَـٰٓئِرِ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ فِيهَا خَيْرٌ ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱسْمَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا صَوَآفَّ ۖ فَإِذَا وَجَبَتْ جُنُوبُهَا فَكُلُوا۟ مِنْهَا وَأَطْعِمُوا۟ ٱلْقَانِعَ وَٱلْمُعْتَرَّ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ سَخَّرْنَـٰهَا لَكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۳٦)
We have appointed for you the [sacrificial] camels as part of Allah’s sacraments. There is good for you in them. So mention the Name of Allah over them as they stand. And when they have fallen on their flanks, eat from them, and feed the self-contained needy and the mendicant. Thus have We disposed them for your benefit so that you may give thanks. (36)

Key Points

Budn (بُدن) is the plural of budnah (بُدنَة), meaning a healthy and fat camel. Sawāff (صَوافّ) refers to an animal that keeps its knee straight (while standing). The phrase wajabat junūbuhā (وَجَبتْ جنوبها) means settling on its side, and is a metaphor for the animal giving up its life (dying). Qāni' (قانع) is a poor person who is content (with what he has), and Mu'tarr (مُعتَرّ) refers to a poor person who is needy but does not speak of it, yet passes by you intending to seek help. [34] In Islam, wherever the issue of eating is raised, another duty is mentioned alongside it:

Kulū... wa lā tusrifū (كُلوا... ولاتسرفوا) [35] (Eat, but do not be extravagant.) Kulū... wa aṭ'imū (كُلوا... واطعموا) [36] (Eat, and feed others.) Kulū... wa'malū (كُلوا... واعلموا) [37] (Eat, and perform righteous deeds.) Kulū... wa shkurū (كُلوا... واشكروا) [38] (Eat, and be grateful.)

34) Tafsir Nūr ath-Thaqalayn; Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 499. 35) Al-A'rāf, 31. 36) Al-Hajj, 36. 37) Al-Mu'minūn, 51. 38) Sabā', 15.

Messages

1- Spend generously in honoring the Divine rituals (Sha'ā'ir). Wal-budn (والبُدن). (Budn means fat camels.)

2- The Arabs of the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah) used to believe that they should not benefit from the meat of the sacrifice. Fakulū (فكلوا) (So eat).

3- In honoring religious rituals, what is important is Allah's attention and care, whether it relates to inanimate objects or animals. Innaṣ-Ṣafā wal-Marwata min sha'ā'irillāh (اِنّ الصفا و المروة من شعائر اللّه), and Wal-budn... min sha'ā'irillāh (والبُدن... من شعائر اللّه).

4- Religious rulings must be determined through revelation. Ja'alnā (جعلنا) (We have appointed).

5- Human benefits are also secured within the Divine rituals. Lakum (لكم) (for you). (Unlike sacrifices made for idols or extravagant waste.)

6- In the educational school of Islam, Allah is relevant even in nutrition. Fadhkurūsmallāh (فاذكروا اسم اللّه) (So mention the Name of Allah).

7- The best method for sacrificing a camel is to slaughter it while it is standing. Ṣawāff (صَوافّ).

8- As long as there is life in the animal's body, consuming its meat is forbidden. Fa idhā wajabat junūbuhā fakulū (فاذا وَجَبت جُنوبها فكلوا) (Then when they fall upon their sides, eat).

9- The meat of the sacrifice must be divided (between yourselves and others). Kulū - aṭ'imū (كلوا - اطعموا) (Eat - feed).

10- Attend to all groups of the destitute. Al-Qāni' - Al-Mu'tarr (القانع - المعترّ).

11- The sacrifice should be performed in a place where the poor pass by. Al-Mu'tarr (المعترّ).

12- The subjugation and submission of animals, the consumption of sacrificial meat and the feeding of others, the slaughtering and sacrificing of animals during the Hajj rituals, and having sincerity and a Divine color—these are blessings for which gratitude must be shown. La'allakum tashkurūn (لعلّكم تشكرون) (that you may be grateful).

Ayah

لَن يَنَالَ ٱللَّهَ لُحُومُهَا وَلَا دِمَآؤُهَا وَلَـٰكِن يَنَالُهُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ مِنكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ سَخَّرَهَا لَكُمْ لِتُكَبِّرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ مَا هَدَىٰكُمْ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۳۷)
It is not their flesh or blood that reaches Allah; rather, it is your piety that reaches Him. Thus has He disposed them for your benefit so that you may magnify Allah for His guiding you. And give good news to the virtuous. (37)

Messages

1- Allah has no need for the duties we perform. «لن یَنالَ اللّه لُحومها»

2- Beyond the apparent matters of religion, there are higher goals. (We should not suffice with the outward appearance of religious commands; having sincerity, the spirit of servitude, and submission to the command of Allah is the inner reality of divine rulings.) «و لكن یَنالُه التّقوى »

3- The relationship between man and Allah is through piety (Taqwa). «یَنالُه التّقوى منكم»

4- Remembering Allah with greatness is a form of divine gratitude (Shukr). «لتكبّروا اللّه على ماهداكم»

5- The blessing of guidance is among the blessings for which special emphasis has been placed on giving thanks. «على ما هداكم»

6- If it were not for the grace of Allah, we would all be misguided. «هداكم»

7- Hajj and its rituals are a clear manifestation of guidance. «على ما هداكم»

8- Performing the rituals of Hajj places man among the righteous doers (Muhsinin). «وبَشّر المحسنین»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُدَٰفِعُ عَنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ خَوَّانٍ كَفُورٍ (۳۸)
Allah will indeed defend those who have faith. Indeed Allah does not like any ingrate traitor. (38)

Key Points

One of the promises and Divine traditions is the support and defense of the believers, and Allah has made this defense and protection incumbent upon Himself as a right: «و كان حَقّاً علینا نَصرالمؤمنین» [39] Of course, the meaning of Divine defense and support is not always immediate and short-term defense and support; rather, it also includes long-term defense, because in other verses He says: «والعاقبة للمتّقین» Yes, it is possible that in a conflict or war, the believers may seemingly be defeated, but their goals and ideology prevail. Just as Ibn Muljam martyred Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, did Allah support him or Ali عليه السلام? The name of Ali, the son of Ali, the book of Ali, the supplications of Ali, the honor of Ali, the Shi'a of Ali, and the ideology of Ali ultimately achieved victory.

«خَوّان» (Khawwān) and «كَفور» (Kafūr) mean someone whose habit and practice are disbelief (kufr) and betrayal.

39) Rūm, 47.

Messages

1- The fulfillment of the Divine promise to defend the believers is certain. «اِنَّ»

2- Believers are not alone and defenseless. «اِنّ اللّه یُدافع عن الّذین آمنوا»

3- Allah's protection is perpetual. «یُدافع»

4- Believers defend the sanctity of God, and God defends the sanctity of the believers. «یُدافع» (Yudāfi‘), not «یدفع» (Yadfa‘).

5- Believers are beloved by Allah. «اِنّ اللّه یُدافع»

6- The element that attracts God's support and defense is faith (Iman). «عن الّذین آمنوا» «و كان حقّاً عَلینا نَصرالمؤمنین» [40]

7- Allah's wrath and anger are based on criteria. «یدافع عن الّذین آمنوا - لایحبّ كل خَوّان» (The religion of Allah is a trust in our hands that must not be betrayed.) «لایحبّ كلّ خَوّان ...»

8- All people are equal before Allah, «كلّ خَوّان» (every betrayer), and betrayal is not forgiven from anyone.

9- Worse than betrayal is habitual betrayal; «خَوّان» (Khawwān). Worse than disbelief (kufr) is habitual disbelief. «كَفور» (Kafūr).

10- Betrayal is the precursor to disbelief. (First «خَوّان» is mentioned, then «كَفور».)

11- Whoever lacks faith has betrayed himself, Allah, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. («خوّان» is placed opposite «آمنوا».)

40) Rūm, 47.

Ayah

أُذِنَ لِلَّذِينَ يُقَـٰتَلُونَ بِأَنَّهُمْ ظُلِمُوا۟ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ نَصْرِهِمْ لَقَدِيرٌ (۳۹)
Those who are fought against are permitted [to fight] because they have been wronged, and Allah is indeed able to help them (39)

Key Points

It has been said that this Ayah is the first Ayah that granted permission for Jihad and armed struggle to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, because His mission had several stages: 1- Invitation (Dawah), spiritual reform, and self-building of forces. 2- Organization and mobilization of forces. 3- Defense or attack.

Messages

1- Jihad is not permissible without the permission of Allah and the Messenger. «اُذِنَ»

2- The oppressed are permitted to wage Jihad against their enemies. «اُذِن - بانّهم ظلموا»

3- Allah's help comes after our rising up and movement. «اُذِن - اِنّ اللّه على نصرهم لقدیر»

4- Give morale to the fighters. «واِنّ اللّه على نَصرهم لقدیر»

5- Truth is victorious over falsehood. «و اِنّ اللّه على نَصرهم لقدیر»

6- Divine aid is not limited only to military aspects but includes all types of help and assistance. «نصرهم» is mentioned in an absolute sense.

7- Divine help is certain. (Indicated by the word «انّ», «لقدیر», the letter lām, and the nominal sentence structure).

8- Allah's aid and support for the believers is extensive. (He used «قدیر» [All-Powerful], not «قادر» [Powerful]).

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِم بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ إِلَّآ أَن يَقُولُوا۟ رَبُّنَا ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ ٱللَّهِ ٱلنَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لَّهُدِّمَتْ صَوَٰمِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَٰتٌ وَمَسَـٰجِدُ يُذْكَرُ فِيهَا ٱسْمُ ٱللَّهِ كَثِيرًا ۗ وَلَيَنصُرَنَّ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَنصُرُهُۥٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَقَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (٤٠)
—those who were expelled from their homes unjustly, only because they said, ‘Allah is our Lord.’ Had not Allah repulsed the people from one another, ruin would have befallen the monasteries, churches, synagogues and mosques in which Allah’s Name is much invoked. Allah will surely help those who help Him. Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (40)

Key Points

The word «صَوامِع» (Sawāmi') is the plural of «صومعه» (Sawma'ah), meaning a monastery, a place of worship and asceticism for monks in deserts and caves. The word «بِیَع» (Biya') is the plural of «بیعه» (Bi'ah), the place of worship for Christians (churches). The word «صَلوات» (Salawāt) is the plural of «صلاة» (Salāh), which is the synagogue and place of worship for Jews. The destruction of a mosque means: the obliteration of unity and organization, cutting ties with the just Imam, forgetting the poor, lack of awareness of current affairs, and heedlessness of Allah. Therefore, if the enemy is able, they first eradicate these centers. «لهُدّمت صَوامع و بِیَع...»

Messages

1- Displacement from one's homeland is the clearest example of oppression. «ظُلموا ... الّذین اُخرجوا»

2- Love of homeland is a natural human right, and expulsion from the homeland is the denial of this right and a cause of oppression. «اُخرجوا من دیارهم»

3- In the Quran, the exile of those who spread corruption on earth is justified, but the exile of believers is unjust. «بغیرحقّ»

4- Piety entails hardship and adversity. «اُخرجوا... الاّ أن یقولوا ربّنا اللّه»

5- Believers are the means by which the Divine Will is realized. «دَفعُ اللّه النّاس بعضهم ببعض» (The defense is from Allah, but carried out by the believers.)

6- To protect religious centers, sometimes blood must be shed. «دَفعُ اللّه النّاس بعضهم ببعض ...»

7- What is important is the defense of the sanctity of mosques, whether through war, or through the pen and speech, or through other programs. «دَفعُ اللّه...»

8- The enemy's most important agenda is the destruction of awakening religious centers. «لَهُدّمت» (Places of worship are a sign of the presence of religion in society.)

9- The name of the place of worship is not important; the disbelievers and aggressors oppose the remembrance of Allah and His path, in whatever form and wherever it may be. «صَوامع - بِیَع - صَلَوات - مساجد»

10- The remembrance of Allah is the main goal and the spirit of actions in religious centers. «یُذكر فیها اسم اللّه»

11- Mosques praised by the Quran are those in which Allah is remembered frequently, and where the minimum is not deemed sufficient. «كثیراً» (Any place where He is remembered more often is more sacred.)

12- Divine aid and victory come after our movement and effort. «لیَنصُرَنّ اللّه مَن ینصره»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ إِن مَّكَّنَّـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَوُا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَمَرُوا۟ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَنَهَوْا۟ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ ۗ وَلِلَّهِ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْأُمُورِ (٤۱)
Those who, if We granted them power in the land, will maintain the prayer, give the zakat, bid what is right and forbid what is wrong. And with Allah rests the outcome of all matters. (41)

Key Points

If power and resources are in the hands of righteous individuals, they utilize them correctly, but if they fall into the hands of the unworthy, they misuse them. Therefore, the world and power are a blessing for one group and a means of misery for another, and the Quran mentions both examples: If the believers attain power, they turn to prayer (Salat), Zakat, and enjoining the good (Amr bil Ma'ruf). But deviated and unworthy people, if they become powerful, their action is transgression, «اِنّ الانسان لَیَطغى » [41] and they step onto the path of destroying economic resources and human progeny, «و اذا تَولّى سَعى فى الارض لیفسد فیها و یُهلك الحَرث و النَّسل» [42] and ultimately, they drag people toward the Fire. «ائمّة یدعون الى النّار» [43]

41) Al-Alaq, 6. 42) Al-Baqarah, 205. 43) Al-Qasas, 41.

Messages

1- The help of Allah comes through the revival of His religion. «و لینصرنّ اللّه من ینصره», «الّذین اِن مَكّنّاهم...»

2- Enjoining the good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) and forbidding the evil (Nahy anil Munkar) requires power. «مكّنّاهم»

3- The victory of the believers prepares the ground for servitude, financial and cultural cooperation, and eliminates heedlessness and arrogance. «اِن مَكّنّاهم ...»

4- For the believers, the entire earth is the same; wherever they have the ability, they implement their sacred goal. «فى الارض»

5- We must consider all powers to be from Allah. «مَكّنّا»

6- The first fruit of the rule of the righteous is Salat (prayer), Zakat, and enjoining the good and forbidding the evil. «اِن مكّناهم فى الارض اَقاموا الصلوة ...»

7- Connection with Allah, helping the deprived, raising awareness in society, and preventing corruption are inseparable. «اَقاموا الصلوة و آتوا الزّكوة و امروا بالمعروف...»

8- Establishing prayer, paying Zakat, and enjoining the good and forbidding the evil are definite duties and actions of the officials of the Islamic government. «اَقاموا» (they established), not «یقیمون» (they establish/will establish).

9- Believing rulers are concerned both with spiritual elevation and the growth of moral affairs, and with welfare, solving economic problems, poverty alleviation, and societal reform. «اَقاموا الصلوة و آتوا الزّكوة ...»

10- A society is under the protection of Allah that possesses these four central values: Salat, Zakat, enjoining the good, and forbidding the evil. «و لینصرن اللّه من ینصره», «الّذین اِن مكنّاهم اَقاموا الصلوة»

11- Your commanding and forbidding is effective in society only when you first fulfill your individual duties. «اقاموا الصلوة... اَمَروا بالمعروف»

12- Establishing prayer requires power and resources. «اِن مكنّاهم ... اقاموا الصلوة»

13- Zakat is the inseparable counterpart of Salat. «اَقاموا الصلوة و آتوا الزكوة» (In 28 verses of the Quran, Zakat is mentioned alongside Salat).

14- Even after fulfilling all duties, do not become certain of your good outcome and the final result of your work, but maintain hope and reliance upon Allah. «وللّه عاقبة الامور»

15- Resources are not in your hands for more than a few days. «وللّه عاقبة الامور»

Ayahs

وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَعَادٌ وَثَمُودُ (٤۲)
If they impugn you, the people of Noah had impugned before them and ‘Ad and Thamud, (42)
وَقَوْمُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَقَوْمُ لُوطٍ (٤۳)
[as well as] the people of Abraham and the people of Lot, (43)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَأَصْحَـٰبُ مَدْيَنَ ۖ وَكُذِّبَ مُوسَىٰ فَأَمْلَيْتُ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ (٤٤)
and the inhabitants of Midian, and Moses was also impugned. But I gave the faithless a respite, then I seized them and how was My rebuttal! (44)

Messages

1- The denial (or rejection) by enemies should not prevent the continuation of the path. «و اِن یُكذّبوك ...»

2- Do not fear the enmity of the disbelievers. «اِن یكذّبوك...» (Allah consoles the Prophet.)

3- History repeats itself. «فقد كُذّبت قبلهم»

4- Use history for lessons and moral development. «قبلهم»

5- Noah, Abraham, and Lot were denied (or rejected) by their own people. (The people of Abraham - the people of Lot - the people of Noah). However, Moses was not denied by his people, but rather by the Copts. «كذّب موسى» (Moses was denied) and He did not say: «قوم موسى» (the people of Moses).

6- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to send Prophets. (Noah, Abraham, Moses, Shu'ayb, and Lot).

7- Allah grants respite to the wicked. «فَأملیت»

8- The wicked should not consider the respite granted by Allah as a sign of His love or His negligence. «ثم أخذتهم»

Ayah

فَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهَا وَهِىَ ظَالِمَةٌ فَهِىَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَبِئْرٍ مُّعَطَّلَةٍ وَقَصْرٍ مَّشِيدٍ (٤٥)
How many towns We have destroyed when they had been wrongdoers! So they lie fallen on their trellises, their wells neglected and their ruined palaces! (45)

Key Points

The word "خاویة" (Khāwiyah) is derived from "خواء" (Khawā'), meaning collapse and falling down. The word "مَشید" (Mashīd) means both a tall palace and a plastered/stuccoed palace.

Messages

1- Allah's wrath upon the oppressors is not an accident; it is a continuous process. "فكأیّن"

2- Learn lessons from history. "فكأیّن من قَریة ..."

3- The result of oppression is destruction. "و هى ظالمة - اهلكناها"

4- Against Allah's wrath, neither a roof nor a pillar remains. "خاویة على عُروشها"

Ayah

أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَتَكُونَ لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ يَعْقِلُونَ بِهَآ أَوْ ءَاذَانٌ يَسْمَعُونَ بِهَا ۖ فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَعْمَى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ وَلَـٰكِن تَعْمَى ٱلْقُلُوبُ ٱلَّتِى فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ (٤٦)
Have they not travelled through the land so that they may have hearts by which they may exercise their reason, or ears by which they may hear? Indeed, it is not the eyes that turn blind, but it is the hearts in the breasts that turn blind! (46)

Messages

1- Academic and experiential journeys are valuable endeavors and desirable means for expanding knowledge. «أفلَم یسیروا»

2- The earth and time are classrooms, and those who do not take heed are deserving of reproach. «أفلَم یسیروا فى الارض»

3- A group of people takes no counsel from external prophets nor from the intellect, which is the internal prophet. «یعقلون بها»

4- Intellect, hearing, and sight are tools of recognition. «یعقلون... آذان... ابصار»

5- Worse than the blindness of the eye is the blindness of the heart, which does not gain sight even by taking counsel. «تعمى القلوب»

Indeed, persistence in obstinacy and enmity toward the truth deforms a person and brings him to a point where he perceives the truth neither through intellect nor through sight and hearing.

Ayah

وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِٱلْعَذَابِ وَلَن يُخْلِفَ ٱللَّهُ وَعْدَهُۥ ۚ وَإِنَّ يَوْمًا عِندَ رَبِّكَ كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ (٤۷)
They ask you to hasten the punishment, though Allah will never break His promise. Indeed a day with your Lord is like a thousand years by your reckoning. (47)

Key Points

In response to the warnings of the prophets, the disbelievers repeatedly asked them: When will that Divine wrath, of which you speak, overtake us? «ویقولون مَتى هذا الوَعد اِن كنتم صادقین» (44). This Ayah answers them by saying: Do not rush; the Divine promise is certain.

(44) This Ayah has been repeated six times in the Quran.

Messages

1- We should not consider Allah's granting of respite as a sign of His negligence or our being abandoned. «یَستعجِلونك»

2- Undue requests, creating a negative atmosphere, and irrelevant questions should not influence us. «یَستَعجِلونك»

3- Time with us is different from time with Allah. «و اِنّ یَوماً عند ربّك»

Ayah

وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَمْلَيْتُ لَهَا وَهِىَ ظَالِمَةٌ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهَا وَإِلَىَّ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٤۸)
To how many a town did I give respite while it was doing wrong! Then I seized it, and toward Me is the destination. (48)

Messages

1- One of the divine traditions is granting respite to the oppressors, so our haste is futile. «و كایّن من قَریة أملَیتُ»

2- Human oppression is the cause of divine wrath. «و هى ظالمة - أخَذتُها»

Ayahs

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٤۹)
Say, ‘O mankind! I am only a manifest warner to you!’ (49)
فَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (٥٠)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, for them will be forgiveness and a noble provision. (50)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ سَعَوْا۟ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا مُعَـٰجِزِينَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (٥۱)
But as for those who contend with Our signs, seeking to frustrate [their purpose], they shall be the inmates of hell. (51)

Messages

1- The prophets did not speak on their own accord. «قل»

2- The warnings of the prophets are for the benefit of the people. «لكم»

3- The Prophet does not possess authority, dominance, or the right to compulsion over the people. «انّما أنَا لكم نذیر مبین»

4- The people's need for warning (inzaar) is greater than their need for glad tidings (tabsheer). «أنَا لكم نذیر»

5- The Prophet speaks clearly to the people. «مبین»

6- Faith is not separate from righteous deeds (both together are the condition for benefiting from divine rewards). «آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات لهم مغفرة ...»

7- Spiritual reward precedes material reward. «مغفرة - رزق كریم»

8- Provision (rizq) is valuable only if it is accompanied by honor and respect. «رزق كریم»

9- No one can prevent the realization of divine goals, even though the disbelievers strive (to do so). «سَعَوا»

10- The disbelievers possess neither new arguments nor logic; their only action is striving to neutralize the path of truth. «مُعاجِزین»

11- Hellfire is eternal for a group of people. «اصحاب الجحیم»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ مِن رَّسُولٍ وَلَا نَبِىٍّ إِلَّآ إِذَا تَمَنَّىٰٓ أَلْقَى ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ فِىٓ أُمْنِيَّتِهِۦ فَيَنسَخُ ٱللَّهُ مَا يُلْقِى ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ ثُمَّ يُحْكِمُ ٱللَّهُ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (٥۲)
We did not send any apostle or prophet before you but that when he recited [the scripture] Satan interjected [something] in his recitation. Thereat Allah nullifies whatever Satan has interjected, and then Allah confirms His signs, and Allah is All-knowing, All-wise. (52)

Key Points

People are divided into several groups regarding the whispers of Satan: 1- Individuals whose souls are affected by Satan's whispers. «یُوَسوسُ فى صُدورالنّاس» (45) 2- Some people whom Satan touches, but they immediately become aware and repel him. «مَسّهم طائفٌ من الشیطان تذكّروا» (46) 3- A group with whom Satan is always present. «فَهُو لَه قَرین» (47) The Divine Prophets do not belong to any of these three categories. They are infallible, and Satan is neither their constant companion, nor does he whisper in their souls, nor does he touch them. Satan does, however, cast suggestions into their plans, programs, and aspirations, and it is clear that the account of the Prophets' plans, programs, and objectives is separate from their personal account.

45) Nas, 5. 46) A'raf, 201. 47) Zukhruf, 36.

Messages

1- The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is the last Prophet. «قبلك» (The word ba'dak [after you] does not exist anywhere in the entire Qur'an concerning the Prophet of Islam).

2- The conflict between truth and falsehood is a continuous process. «مِن رسول و لانبىّ الاّ اذا تَمنّى ألقَى الشیطان...»

3- Not all Prophets are on the same level. «رسول - نبىّ» (A Rasul [Messenger] is responsible for widespread propagation, but a Nabi [Prophet] is not to that extent).

4- Prophets have plans and programs for their objectives. «تَمنّى»

5- Satan has no effect on the Prophets themselves, but he whispers concerning their objectives. «اُمنیّته»

6- Satan's whispers threaten even the best programs. «اُمنیّته»

7- In the conflict between truth and falsehood, Allah obliterates falsehood and establishes the truth. «فیَنسَخ اللّه ... ثمّ یحكم»

8- Do not rush the victory of truth over falsehood. («ثمّ» [then] is a symbol of the long term.)

9- Truth is not sustainable without the support of Allah. «یُحكم اللّه آیاته»

10- The conflict between truth and falsehood and the victory of truth over falsehood is a wise and knowledgeable Divine plan. «یُحكم اللّه - علیم حكیم»

Ayah

لِّيَجْعَلَ مَا يُلْقِى ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ فِتْنَةً لِّلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَٱلْقَاسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ لَفِى شِقَاقٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٥۳)
That He may make what Satan has thrown in a test for those in whose hearts is a sickness and those whose hearts have hardened. Indeed the wrongdoers are steeped in extreme defiance. (53)

Messages

1- Satan's insinuations are a means of testing hard-hearted and sick-hearted individuals. «فِتنة» (Spiritual sickness and hard-heartedness are suitable grounds for falling into the trap of trials and Satan.)

2- Although hard-heartedness is a type of spiritual sickness, it is mentioned separately so that its importance may be highlighted. «والقاسیة قلوبهم»

3- Hard-hearted individuals who fall under the influence of Satan's insinuations are oppressors. «واِنّ الظالمین»

Ayah

وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَتُخْبِتَ لَهُۥ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهَادِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٥٤)
[And also for the reason] that those who have been given knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, and so they may have faith in it, and their hearts may be humbled before Him. Indeed Allah guides those who have faith to a straight path. (54)

Key Points

The infiltrations of Satan into the programs of the Prophets, and their subsequent abrogation by Allah, have two effects: 1- They serve as a means of trial for the sick-hearted and the hard-hearted. 2- The neutralization of Satan's suggestions leads to the illumination of the heart and the strengthening of the faith of those possessing knowledge and awareness.

Messages

1- Knowledge is that which gives a person the power to distinguish truth from falsehood. (And it should not merely be superficial or imitative memorization). «و لیعلم اَنّه الحقّ...»

2- Sometimes material or spiritual benefits and understandings relate to the personal disposition and character of individuals. A single satanic suggestion is a trial (fitnah) for the hard-hearted, but for the people of knowledge, it becomes a key to recognition. «لیعلم»

3- Knowledge is a divine gift that is bestowed upon individuals. «اوتوا»

4- The people of knowledge should not be hard-hearted or sick-hearted. This is because in this Ayah, «اوتوا العلم» is placed in contrast to «فى قلوبهم مرض والقاسیة قلوبهم» — which appeared in the previous Ayah.

5- Knowledge is from Him «اوتوا العلم», the Truth is from Him «الحقّ من ربّك», and guidance is also from Him. «انّ اللّه لهاد الّذین»

6- Knowledge, faith, and humility (submission) are stages of human perfection. «اوتواالعلم، فیؤمنوا، فتُخبِت»

7- Whoever takes the first step of faith, Allah guides him to the subsequent steps. «فیؤمنوا... اِنّ اللّه لهاد الّذین»

Ayah

وَلَا يَزَالُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فِى مِرْيَةٍ مِّنْهُ حَتَّىٰ تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَقِيمٍ (٥٥)
Those who are faithless persist in their doubt about it, until either the Hour overtakes them suddenly, or they are overtaken by the punishment of an inauspicious day. (55)

Messages

1- If doubt and skepticism become the prelude to investigation and faith, they are valuable. It is perpetual doubt and skepticism whose end is deviation and punishment. «لایَزال»

2- Do not expect faith from all people, because for a group of them, no proof or argument is effective. «لایَزال ... فى مِریَة»

3- Whoever accepts the path of disbelief and fighting against the truth deals with suspicion and doubt in the face of every truth. «الّذین كفروا فى مِریَة»

4- Since death and the Resurrection are sudden, let us never be heedless. «بَغتَة»

5- After the establishment of the Resurrection, there is no opportunity to compensate for our past. «عَقیم»

Ayah

ٱلْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلَّهِ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ فَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فِى جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (٥٦)
On that day all sovereignty will belong to Allah: He will judge between them; then those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be in gardens of bliss, (56)

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, no one will possess any power except by His will. «المُلك یومئذ للّه»

2- Allah's judgment is based on our performance. «یُحكم... فالّذین آمنوا... جنّات»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (٥۷)
and those who are faithless and deny Our signs—for such there will be a humiliating punishment. (57)

Messages

1- The basis of Allah's judgment on the Day of Resurrection is the disbelief and faith of the people. «یُحكم... فالّذین آمنوا... و الّذین كفروا»

2- The punishment for the one who arrogantly rejects the truth is a humiliating torment. «والّذین كفروا و كذّبوا... لهم عذاب مُهین»

Ayahs
