وَٱلَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلُوٓا۟ أَوْ مَاتُوا۟ لَيَرْزُقَنَّهُمُ ٱللَّهُ رِزْقًا حَسَنًا ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (٥۸)
Those who migrate in the way of Allah and then are slain, or die, Allah will surely provide them with a good provision. Allah is indeed the best of providers. (58)
لَيُدْخِلَنَّهُم مُّدْخَلًا يَرْضَوْنَهُۥ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلِيمٌ حَلِيمٌ (٥۹)
He will admit them into an abode they are pleased with. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing. (59)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Goal-oriented migration (Hijrah) is a value. «والّذین هاجروا»

2- The death of migrants is equivalent to martyrdom. «قُتِلوا أو ماتوا» (Death or martyrdom is not important; what is important is being in the way of Allah.)

3- There is no failure in the way of Allah; if the worthy servants of Allah are deprived of worldly pleasures, they will attain the great blessings of the Hereafter. «لیَرزُقنّهم»

4- The fulfillment of divine promises is certain. «لیَرزُقنّهم» (The letter lām and the letter nūn [indicating emphasis/certainty].)

5- Worldly provision (Rizq) is sometimes good and sometimes bitter, but the people of Paradise enjoy only good provision. «رِزقاً حَسَنا»

6- The true Provider (Rāziq) is Allah, and others are merely intermediaries for provision. «خیر الرّازقین»

7- A pleasant dwelling and abode is among the most prominent examples of good provision. «رِزقاً حَسَناً... مُدخلا یَرضونه»

Ayah

۞ ذَٰلِكَ وَمَنْ عَاقَبَ بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبَ بِهِۦ ثُمَّ بُغِىَ عَلَيْهِ لَيَنصُرَنَّهُ ٱللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌ (٦٠)
So will it be; and whoever retaliates with the like of what he has been made to suffer, and is aggressed against [again] thereafter, Allah will surely help him. Indeed Allah is all-excusing, all-forgiving. (60)

Key Points

In Tafsir Namuneh, we read: The polytheists said during the month of Muharram: Since the Muslims consider fighting in this month forbidden, we should attack them because they will not defend themselves. However, this Ayah states that self-defense is obligatory, regardless of the month.

Messages

1- Defense is the natural right of every human being. «و مَن عاقب بمثل ما عُوقِبَ به»

2- Justice is a value everywhere. «بمثل ما عُوقِب»

3- The Helper of the oppressed is Allah. «لینصُرَنّه اللّه»

4- Aiding the oppressed is not always accompanied by the destruction of the oppressor. Allah helps the oppressed, but the oppressor might also be granted Divine forgiveness for some reason. «لَینصُرَنّه اللّه - عفوٌّ غَفور»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (٦۱)
So will it be, because Allah makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night, and because Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. (61)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah said: "لَيَنصُرَنَّهُ" (He will surely help him), meaning We help the oppressed. In this Ayah and the next Ayah, He refers to the reasons and foundations for this help, which consist of: 1- The power of Allah ("يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ") 2- His knowledge ("سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ") 3- His being the Truth ("ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ") 4- His greatness and majesty ("الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ").

Messages

1- A handful is a sample of the whole heap. The power and knowledge of Allah in the changing of night and day is a sign of His power to grant help to His servants. "يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ"

2- The changes of night and day are not accidental, and the management of the affairs of the world has a Wise and Knowing Director. "يُولِجُ ..."

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَأَنَّ مَا يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ هُوَ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (٦۲)
So will it be, because Allah is the Reality, and what they invoke besides Him is nullity, and because Allah is the All-exalted, the All-great. (62)

Messages

1- The alternation of night and day is an eloquent sign of His planning, Lordship, and Oneness. «ذلك باَنّ اللّه هو الحقّ»

2- Every object of worship besides Him is illusory. «باَنّ اللّه هو الحقّ ... من دونه هو الباطل»

3- Almighty God is the criterion of truth, not anything else. «ذلك باَنّ اللّه هو الحقّ»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَتُصْبِحُ ٱلْأَرْضُ مُخْضَرَّةً ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌ (٦۳)
Have you not regarded that Allah sends down water from the sky, whereupon the earth turns green? Indeed Allah is all-attentive, all-aware. (63)

Messages

1- Studying existence is the foundation for faith in Allah. «ألم تَر» (Have you not seen)

2- Allah operates in the universe based on natural factors. «أنزل من السماء ماءً فتُصبِح» (He sends down water from the sky, and then the earth becomes)

3- The effects and blessings of nature are from Him. (If rain makes the dead earth green and lush, this is also divine grace). «فتُصبِح - اِنّ اللّه لطیف» (it becomes - Indeed, Allah is Kind/Subtle)

Ayah

لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ (٦٤)
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, Indeed Allah is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable. (64)

Key Points

The existence of man is entirely poverty, and he is in need of air, food, shelter, clothing, and other things. If for a few days he considers himself independent in some aspect of his affairs, he resorts to transgression, which is followed by consequences and dangers, such as: indifference, heedlessness, and exploitation. But Allah, «الغنىّ الحمید» (The Self-Sufficient, The Praiseworthy), is the Absolute Independent and worthy of all praise.

Messages

1- Creation is needy and dependent, and He alone is the Self-Sufficient. «اِنّ اللّه لهوالغنىّ...»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلْفُلْكَ تَجْرِى فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِۦ وَيُمْسِكُ ٱلسَّمَآءَ أَن تَقَعَ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِٱلنَّاسِ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٦٥)
Have you not regarded that Allah has disposed for you [r benefit] whatever there is in the earth, and [that] the ships sail at sea by His command, and He sustains the sky lest it should fall on the earth, excepting [when it does so] by His leave? Indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to mankind. (65)

Key Points

The meaning of the subjugation (of creation) is that everything has been placed in the path of human benefit and enjoyment. For example, the sun is subjugated to us, meaning we benefit from it. Mentioning the ship alongside the earthly blessings is due to its important role. Even today, if ships were to stop for a single day, the wheel of the world's economy would be paralyzed.

Messages

1- Studying creation generates both faith and love. «ألم تَر»

2- The subjugation of the universe is by the Will of Allah. «اِنّ اللّه سَخّر لكم»

3- Man can dominate the earth and its phenomena, and this is his legitimate right. «سخّر لكم» (Man is the noblest of the earth's phenomena).

4- The course of nature is in the path of God's Will. «تَجرى بامره»

5- The sky and its celestial bodies are prone to falling, and Allah holds them back. «یُمسِك»

6- The establishment of the system of creation and its immunity from collapse require the constant preservation of Allah. «یُمسِك»

7- Natural laws do not impede the Divine Will. «الاّ باذنه»

8- The reason for Divine favors is His compassion and grace. «اِنّ اللّه بالنّاس لرؤف رحیم»

The subservience of nature to man, the movement of ships in the sea, and the security of the earth from collision with celestial bodies are manifestations of Allah's vast compassion and mercy. «سَخّر لكم... تَجرى فى البحر ... یُمسِك السماء ... لرؤف رحیم»

9- All people are subject to the grace of Allah. «بالنّاس لرؤف ...»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْيَاكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَكَفُورٌ (٦٦)
It is He who gave you life then He makes you die, then He brings you to life. Indeed man is very ungrateful. (66)

Messages

1- Despite the fact that death, life, and the past, present, and future of man are in the hands of Allah, man still rebels and is ungrateful. «أَحْيَاكُمْ... إِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ لَكَفُورٌ»

Ayah

لِّكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ جَعَلْنَا مَنسَكًا هُمْ نَاسِكُوهُ ۖ فَلَا يُنَـٰزِعُنَّكَ فِى ٱلْأَمْرِ ۚ وَٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ هُدًى مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٦۷)
For every nation We have appointed rites [of worship] which they observe; so let them not dispute with you concerning your religion, and invite to your Lord. Indeed, you are on a straight guidance. (67)

Key Points

Since humans have seasonal, social, and non-fixed needs, in addition to general and fixed commands, new commands must be issued in different circumstances, just as a doctor changes his prescriptions according to the patient's various conditions, and in our time, jurists (Fuqaha) determine new issues for Muslims within the framework of general and fixed laws. Some have taken "مَنسَك" (mansak) as a verbal noun meaning "نُسك" (nusuk) and sacrificing, which makes the meaning of the Ayah: We appointed a type of sacrifice for every nation, which they performed. Therefore, the polytheists should not find fault with your program of sacrifice.

Messages

1- Allah does not leave any nation without a school of thought (or divine law). «لكلّ اُمّة»

2- Religion must be from God (Allah). «جعلنا»

3- People must tread the divine path. «هم ناسِكوه»

4- The duty of the preacher is the guidance of the nation and steadfastness in this path. «وادعُ الى ربّك»

5- The correct path is the path of the Prophets. «اِنّك لعَلى هدًى»

6- In the face of dispute, contention, and the weakening efforts of opponents, strengthening the leaders of truth is necessary. «فلا ینازعنّك فى الامر... اِنّك لعلى هُدىً مستقیم»

7- Whoever is responsible for guiding others must have mastery over the true and straight path. «لعَلى هدىً مستقیم», «انّك لَمِن المرسلین عَلى صراط مستقیم» (48) (The word "عَلى" (upon) is the symbol of mastery/dominance.)

48) Ya Sin, 3 to 4.

Ayahs

وَإِن جَـٰدَلُوكَ فَقُلِ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (٦۸)
But if they dispute with you, say, ‘Allah knows best what you are doing. (68)
ٱللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (٦۹)
Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which you used to differ. (69)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Allah consoles and instructs His Prophet. «و اِن جادَلوك فقل»

2- Despite all their miracles and logic, the Prophets were still afflicted by obstinate individuals. «واِن جادَلوك»

3- Remembering Allah and entrusting affairs to Him is the best means of achieving tranquility. «و اِن جادَلوك فقل اللّه اعلم ...»

4- Do not engage in conflict with obstinate people and those who love argumentation. «و اِن جادَلوك فقل اللّه أعلَم»

5- On the Day of Resurrection, the Judge and Ruler is the Almighty God, Who is the Witness and the Knower. «اللّه أعلَم - اللّه یحكم» (Perfect judgment is based on perfect knowledge.)

6- Belief in Allah's knowledge and judgment is a source of tranquility for the believers and a threat to the disbelievers. «اللّه أعلَم - اللّه یحكم»

7- Belief in the Day of Resurrection is the best leverage for controlling disputes and arguments. «اللّه یحكم یوم القیامة»

8- The conflict between the front of truth and falsehood has a very long history. «كنتم فیه تختلفون» (The word «كنتم» combined with the imperfect verb indicates a long history.)

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ فِى كِتَـٰبٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ (۷٠)
Do you not know that Allah knows whatever there is in the heaven and the earth? All that is in a Book. That is indeed easy for Allah.’ (70)

Messages

1- Allah's knowledge concerning all existence is uniform. «یَعلم ما فى السماء والارض»

2- Allah's knowledge is recorded in a book. «فى كتاب» (Alternatively, the deeds that you perform, which were referred to in the two preceding verses, are recorded in the book.)

3- Allah's comprehensive knowledge encompassing all matters and recording it in a specific book is easy for Him. «على اللّه یَسیر»

Ayah

وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِۦ سُلْطَـٰنًا وَمَا لَيْسَ لَهُم بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ ۗ وَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِن نَّصِيرٍ (۷۱)
They worship besides Allah that for which He has not sent down any authority, and of which they have no knowledge. The wrongdoers will have no helper. (71)

Key Points

The view of the polytheists was that Allah had granted the power of administration and decision-making to certain objects and idols, but He Himself was above all of them. They imagined that the universe was under the administration of gods (lords/arbaab) and they considered Allah to be the "Lord of Lords" (رب الارباب) and the God of gods. This Ayah states: Allah has not given any authority whatsoever to any of your imaginary deities, and existence is under the administration of the One God. «یَعبدون من دون اللّه مالم یُنزّل به سُلطانا» Perhaps the intent of the "نزول سلطان" (sending down authority) in the Ayah is scientific/intellectual dominance. Meaning, the polytheists turn to deities and idols for which Allah has never sent down any proof or evidence based on knowledge, logic, or revelation in their support.

Messages

1- The motivation for worship is either a divine command: «یُنزّل به سلطانا», or the guidance of intellect and knowledge: «لیس لهم به علم», or the hope of His help and assistance: «ما للظّالمین من نصیر». The polytheists possess none of these.

2- Any worship that lacks evidence from revelation or knowledge is oppression (zulm). «و ما للظّالمین»

Ayah

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ تَعْرِفُ فِى وُجُوهِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلْمُنكَرَ ۖ يَكَادُونَ يَسْطُونَ بِٱلَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا ۗ قُلْ أَفَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِشَرٍّ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمُ ۗ ٱلنَّارُ وَعَدَهَا ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۷۲)
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, you perceive denial on the faces of the faithless: they would almost pounce upon those who recite Our signs to them. Say, ‘Shall I inform you about something worse than that? The Fire which Allah has promised the faithless, and it is an evil destination.’ (72)

Key Points

The word "yastūna" (یَسطون) is derived from "satw" (سطو), meaning attack and aggression. In the struggle between disbelief (kufr) and faith (īmān), the sharp edge of the disbelievers' attacks is focused on spiritual and cultural centers and personalities. Sometimes they target places of worship, as we read in Ayah 40 of this very Sūrah: «لَهُدّمت صَوامِع وبِیَع» (monasteries and churches would certainly have been demolished). And sometimes they seek to attack the preachers and propagators of the religion or their listeners. «یَسطون بالّذین یَتلون»

Messages

1- Disbelievers are always in a state of denial. «اذا تُتلى... تَعرِفُ فى وجوه الّذین كفروا المنكر»

2- Allah establishes the conclusive proof (itmām al-ḥujjah) against the disbelievers. «تُتلى علیهم»

3- The proof and argument must be clear. «بَیّنات»

4- The malice and hatred of the disbelievers do not remain hidden. «تَعرِفُ - یَسطون»

5- Inner and spiritual denial affects the physical body. «فى وجوه»

6- Whoever lacks proof resorts to attack and assault. «یَسطون» (Resorting to force is a sign of logical failure.)

7- Preachers or converts to the religion should prepare themselves for the attacks of the opponents. «یَسطون بالّذین یَتلون»

8- Enlightening religious propagation is a cold fire upon the souls of the disbelievers. «بِشَرٍّ من ذلكم النّار»

9- When warning and threatening stubborn individuals, mention the Fire (Hell). «النّار»

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ضُرِبَ مَثَلٌ فَٱسْتَمِعُوا۟ لَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ لَن يَخْلُقُوا۟ ذُبَابًا وَلَوِ ٱجْتَمَعُوا۟ لَهُۥ ۖ وَإِن يَسْلُبْهُمُ ٱلذُّبَابُ شَيْـًٔا لَّا يَسْتَنقِذُوهُ مِنْهُ ۚ ضَعُفَ ٱلطَّالِبُ وَٱلْمَطْلُوبُ (۷۳)
O people! Listen to a parable that is being drawn: Indeed those whom you invoke besides Allah will never create [even] a fly even if they all rallied to do so! And if a fly should take away something from them, they can not recover that from it. Feeble is the seeker and the sought! (73)
مَا قَدَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَقَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (۷٤)
They do not regard Allah with the regard due to Him. Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (74)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- The manner of address must be appropriate for the audience. «یا ایّهاالنّاس - تَدعون من دون اللّه» (The polytheists are not at a stage where they should be addressed with anything better than «یا ایّها النّاس» [O mankind]).

2- In guidance and propagation (Da'wah), we should first prepare people to listen, and then speak. «ضُرِبَ مَثلٌ فاستَمعوا له»

3- We should pay close attention and be precise regarding the Quranic parables. «فاستَمِعوا له»

4- Even if human beings employ all their power, they cannot create a living creature. «لن یَخلُقوا ذُباباً ولو اجتمعوا له»

5- We should not underestimate small creatures. «اِن یَسلُبهم الذُّباب»

6- He who is powerless against a single fly is not worthy of being worshipped. «لایَستَنقِذوه منه»

7- The polytheists accepted Allah, but they also worshipped others besides Him. (The correct appreciation of Allah is monotheism). «ما قدروا اللّه حقّ قَدرِه»

8- The motivation for polytheism (Shirk) is seeking help from partners, but one must abandon all besides Allah and turn only to Him, for power and might belong exclusively to Allah. «اِنّ اللّه لَقوىّ عزیز»

Ayahs

ٱللَّهُ يَصْطَفِى مِنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ رُسُلًا وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (۷٥)
Allah chooses messengers from angels and from mankind. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. (75)
يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۗ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (۷٦)
He knows that which is before them and that which is behind them, and to Allah all matters are returned. (76)

Key Points

The word "اِصطَفى" (Iṣṭafā) is derived from "صَفوَة" (Ṣafwah), meaning pure and unadulterated. The selection of the pure indicates that some people and angels possess merit and are pure and unadulterated. Abu Dharr narrates from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that the number of prophets (Anbiyā) is one hundred and twenty-four thousand, of whom three hundred and thirteen were Messengers (Rusul). (49) Allah's angels are not all the same; their status and their responsibilities differ. The status of some, such as Gabriel (Jibrīl), Michael (Mīkā'īl), and Azrael ('Izrā'īl), is superior to others.

49) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 3, p. 104 and Vol. 4, p. 452.

Messages

1- The selection of the Prophet is the right of Allah, Who is the Creator and the All-Seeing (Baṣīr). "يَصْطَفِي - بَصِير" (Perhaps the words All-Hearing (Samī') and All-Seeing (Baṣīr) symbolize that whoever We choose and entrust with responsibility, We supervise and support their work.)

2- Not everyone possesses the merit and capacity for divine message delivery. "مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ رُسُلًا وَمِنَ النَّاسِ"

3- The universe is in the presence of Allah. "يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ"

4- Now that Allah knows, and we are all returning to Him, we should not commit sins. "يَعْلَمُ... تُرْجَعُ"

5- Allah has created creation in such a way that the return of all matters is to Him. "وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ"

6- The movement of existence toward Allah does not depend on their willingness or unwillingness. "تُرْجَعُ"

7- The caravan of existence is moving toward a specific goal. "تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱرْكَعُوا۟ وَٱسْجُدُوا۟ وَٱعْبُدُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ وَٱفْعَلُوا۟ ٱلْخَيْرَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ ۩ (۷۷)
O you who have faith! Bow down and prostrate yourselves, and worship your Lord, and do good, so that you may be felicitous. (77)

Key Points

Although Ruku' (bowing) and Sujud (prostration) are instances of worship ('Ibadah), their names are mentioned in this Ayah alongside "واعبدوا" (and worship), which indicates the importance of Salat (prayer) and these two pillars of prayer. Two programs are presented for human happiness, and his salvation lies in performing both types of actions: A: Fixed programs, such as Ruku', Sujud, and other acts of worship. B: Variable programs dependent on time and place, where good deeds (Khayr) have a specific manifestation in every era. "آمنوا اركعوا... وافعلوا الخیر" (Believe, bow... and do good).

Messages

1- Good deeds (including innovations, inventions, and services) are effective only when they are under the shadow of faith and servitude to Allah. "آمنوا اركعوا ... وافعلوا الخیر"

2- Even with Ruku', Sujud, devotion, and good deeds, do not consider your salvation certain, because the affliction of arrogance and the nullification of deeds are possible. "لعلّكم تفلحون"

3- Falah (success/salvation) is the final stage of perfection, which must be hoped for after performing acts of worship and good deeds. "لعلّكم تفلحون"

Ayah

وَجَـٰهِدُوا۟ فِى ٱللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِۦ ۚ هُوَ ٱجْتَبَىٰكُمْ وَمَا جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِى ٱلدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ ۚ مِّلَّةَ أَبِيكُمْ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ۚ هُوَ سَمَّىٰكُمُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ مِن قَبْلُ وَفِى هَـٰذَا لِيَكُونَ ٱلرَّسُولُ شَهِيدًا عَلَيْكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ ۚ فَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَٱعْتَصِمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ هُوَ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ ۖ فَنِعْمَ ٱلْمَوْلَىٰ وَنِعْمَ ٱلنَّصِيرُ (۷۸)
And wage jihad for the sake of Allah, a jihad which is worthy of Him. He has chosen you and has not placed for you any obstacle in the religion, the faith of your father, Abraham. He named you ‘Muslims’ before, and in this, so that the Apostle may be a witness to you, and that you may be witnesses to mankind. So maintain the prayer, give the zakat, and hold fast to Allah. He is your master—an excellent master and an excellent helper. (78)

Key Points

We know that bearing witness requires knowledge and justice (adalah). Not all people are just or knowledgeable enough to be witnesses over others. Therefore, the intent behind the Muslims bearing witness, as mentioned in this Ayah, refers to a select group among them who possess both knowledge of people's deeds and conduct, and who are trustworthy and just. Such individuals, as we read in the narrations (Riwāyāt), are the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet and the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), to whom our deeds and conduct are presented.

At the beginning of this Surah, Allah mentioned the great earthquake of the Day of Judgment and the severe Divine punishment. At the end of the Surah, He spoke of Jihad, prayer (Salat), and poor-due (Zakat), reminding us that He is the excellent Guardian (Mawla), Protector, and Helper for all His servants. This point teaches us that to be saved from the dangers of the great earthquake of the Day of Judgment and the severe Divine punishment, we must be His servants and seek refuge in Him, for He is «نِعم المَولى و نعم النصیر» (the Excellent Guardian and the Excellent Helper).

The negation of haraj (hardship/difficulty) does not mean that Divine rulings are not difficult, because in this very Ayah, He commands: "Strive in the way of Allah as is His due striving." It is obvious that Jihad is an extremely difficult task. Therefore, the intent behind the absence of haraj in religion is that Islam does not lead to a dead end. For example, in the command for Jihad, the sick, the lame, the blind, the elderly, women, children, and the disabled are exempt, but defense remains a duty despite all its difficulties.

Messages

1- The value of deeds depends on their motive, method, and quantity. (Jihad is valuable only when it is performed with full effort in the way of Allah.) «فى اللّه حقّ جهاده»

2- Both grant dignity to people, «اِجتَباكم - أبیكم - تَكونوا شهداء على الناس», and do not impose hardship. «ما جَعَل علیكم فى الدّین من حَرَج»

3- When legislating, we must observe the capacity of the people. «ما جَعل علیكم فى الدّین من حَرَج»

4- Islam is an easy religion, and its rulings are flexible. It is suitable for implementation in various times and places. «ما جَعل علیكم فى الدّین من حَرَج»

5- Islam is the religion of Abraham (Ibrahimi) and has a very brilliant history. «هو سَمّاكم المسلمین»

6- Prophets are the fathers of the nations (Ummahs). «أبیكم»

7- "Muslim" is a blessed name that was chosen for us beforehand. «مِن قَبل»

8- The Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) is a witness over our deeds. «لیكون الرّسول شهیدا»

9- Prayer (Salat) and poor-due (Zakat) are expressions of gratitude to Allah. (In gratitude for the selection, history, worthiness, and witnessing role of the Islamic Ummah, prayer must be established.) «فاقیموا الصلوة...»

10- We should not rely solely on being chosen or on the name of Islam and Muslim identity; we must safeguard these honors through prayer, poor-due, and holding fast to Allah. «فاقیموا الصلوة و آتواالزكوة»

11- The legitimacy of human guardianships (Wilāyāt) and governments depends on their connection to Divine Guardianship. «هو مولاكم»

«الحمدللّه ربّ العالمین»

Portrait of Surah Al-Mu'minun

This Surah, which has 118 verses (Ayahs), was revealed in Mecca.

Given the description of the characteristics of the believers (Mu'minūn) and the signs of faith in the opening verses of this Surah, the name "Al-Mu'minun" (The Believers) was chosen for it. Furthermore, the stories of Prophets such as Noah, Hud, Moses, and Jesus (علیهم السلام) and the responses of various nations to their calls are presented later in the Surah.

Regarding the virtue of reciting this Surah, Imam Sadiq (علیه السلام) states: Continuous recitation of this Surah, especially on Fridays, leads to happiness in this world and the Hereafter, and companionship with the Divine Prophets in the highest Paradise (Firdaws). [50]

50) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān.
